title: Graduate Civil Engineer
description: The Colas Group operates internationally in all aspects of transport infrastructure. As part of the global organisation, Colas Ireland is primarily involved the distribution of bitumen products, emulsion and emulsifier manufacturing, and the provision of specialist road maintenance services on the national, regional and local road network. In Ireland Colas operates from 14 locations across the island of Ireland. **Graduate Civil Engineering Programme** **International Graduate Programme – Civil Engineering** **Build Your Global Career with a Market Leader** At **Colas Ireland** , part of the world‑renowned Bouygues Group, we don’t just build roads — we build careers, leaders, and opportunities that span the globe. With over 100 years of excellence and operations in **54 countries** , we are a market leader in delivering innovative, sustainable infrastructure projects. The **Colas GPS Graduate Programme** is a **2‑year, fully funded** international journey for ambitious Civil Engineering graduates ready to work on cutting‑edge projects and fast‑track their careers. **What You’ll Get** * €37,500 starting salary plus potential performance bonus. * 3 rotations across 3 countries – 6 months in Ireland (Dublin, Galway, Ennis or Athlone) followed by 1\.5 years international assignments. * Hands‑on responsibilities that put you at the heart of large‑scale projects. * Training, mentoring, and exposure to senior industry experts. * A guaranteed pathway to a full‑time role on completion. **Your Role** From your first day, you’ll play a key part in managing site operations, tracking project milestones, and collaborating with teams to deliver world‑class infrastructure. Whether it’s bridges, high‑tech facilities, or iconic transport networks, you’ll work on projects that shape communities and make an impact. **Who We’re Looking For** * Recent Civil Engineering graduates with a passion for infrastructure and sustainability. * Adaptable, curious problem‑solvers keen to embrace new cultures and challenges. * Fluent in English (other languages welcome) and willing to travel. **How It Works** Start in Ireland for 6 months, then embark on 1\.5 years fully funded international rotations — gaining invaluable global experience and joining a network of professionals across multiple continents. **This is your chance to join a market leader with a reputation for excellence, international mobility, and a commitment to developing future leaders.** Apply now and start your global journey with Colas Ireland 2026\. *At Colas, our culture is guided by four core values: **Respect** , **Commitment** , **Pioneering** , and **Sharing** . These values shape how we work, collaborate, and grow together. We believe that **respect** is the foundation of every relationship. By treating others as we wish to be treated, we foster fairness, transparency, and inclusion within our teams and with our clients, communities, and partners. **Commitment** means dedicating ourselves fully to everything we do. We strive to create an environment where everyone feels valued, supported, and empowered to make a meaningful impact. With a **pioneering** spirit, we prioritise bold innovation over blind ambition. We welcome new ideas, invest in our people, and work to deliver sustainable infrastructure solutions that shape the future. Through **sharing** , we build trust and solidarity, encouraging collaboration and growth at all levels of the business.* *Colas Group is committed to building a diverse and inclusive workforce that mirrors the communities we serve. As an equal opportunity employer, we welcome applications from all backgrounds. If you want a workplace where your voice is valued and your potential is developed, Colas is the right choice.*

title: Graduate Development Programme
description: Are you an aspiring engineer looking for a supportive environment to grow your career? As one of Ireland’s leading engineering consultancies, we invite you to apply for our Graduate Development Programme. As a fully Irish\-owned firm specializing in the planning, engineering design and delivery in the property, infrastructure, environmental energy sectors, with over 37 years’ experience in the industry, DBFL offers a unique opportunity to kickstart your career. By joining our team, you can become part of our legacy of excellence and innovation, working alongside some of the most skilled professionals in the industry. Our Graduate Development Programme is specifically designed to support your growth from graduate level to Chartership over a period of four years. From the moment you step through our doors, you will be immersed in a dynamic, supportive and collaborative environment that encourages you to learn and excel. You will receive hands\-on experience through a range of exciting projects and benefit from a structured training programme that includes mentorship, internal training sessions, educational site visits, external workshops, and self\-directed learning opportunities. This comprehensive approach ensures that you develop the skills and competencies necessary to thrive in as your engineering career develops. At DBFL, we understand that the journey to becoming a Chartered Engineer is a significant milestone in your professional development. As an Engineers Ireland CPD Accredited organization, we are committed to providing you with the resources and support needed to achieve Chartership. Our programme aligns with the current requirements of Engineers Ireland, ensuring that all areas of competency and core skills are thoroughly covered. With our guidance and your dedication, you will be well on your way to obtaining this prestigious professional title within a minimum of four years. We believe that a successful career is built not only on technical expertise but also on a supportive and inclusive work environment. With a Silver Investors in Diversity accreditation from The Irish Centre for Diversity, DBFL is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, fostering a culture where every team member is valued and heard. Our modern, open\-plan offices in all our locations, promote collaboration and innovation, and our active social calendar ensures that you will have plenty of opportunities to build strong professional relationships and enjoy your time at DBFL. We strive to create a workplace where you can thrive both professionally and personally. To be considered for the programme you will need the following: * Bachelor’s or Masters’ Degree in Civil /Environmental Engineering * A good understanding of civil principles * Good written and oral communication skills * Excellent analytical, critical thinking and strategic skills * Good standards of accuracy and attention to detail * Good Computer skills * ‘A looking to learn’ attitude * Works well on own initiative and as part of a team Are you ready to take the first step towards an exciting and fulfilling career with DBFL? To be considered for our Graduate Development Programme, you should have a Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Civil, Structural or Transportation Engineering, strong analytical and critical thinking skills, and a keen eye for detail. We value good communication skills and a proactive, team\-oriented attitude. Join us at DBFL, where your engineering journey begins, and where you can become part of our mission, Engineering Sustainable Futures.

title: Graduate Mechanical Engineer
description: Graduate Mechanical Engineer, Ballycoolin Business Park, Dublin Apply Now ***Mercury is the European leader in construction solutions.*** We build and manage complex engineering \& construction projects for the world’s leading corporations. Our solutions help deliver technologies and life changing advancements that connect people, communities, and businesses, giving them the power to achieve incredible things. Our people have the courage to be innovative. Their determination and sharp focus enable us to deliver with certainty, time and time again. We deliver our clients’ visions through leading edge construction solutions across multiple sectors, including Enterprise Data Centres, Advanced Technology \& Life Sciences, Hyperscale Data Centres, Fire Protection. At Mercury, it is our duty to encourage and back our people to realise their vision of themselves. We place them at the heart of what we do, providing challenging opportunities to develop within a great team in a supportive environment that allows them to reach their full potential. **Our Graduate Programme** We’re seeking the leaders of tomorrow to join us on our Graduate Programme \- those who are willing to share our values, live our culture and deliver beyond expectations. At Mercury we know that to achieve incredible things you must invest in your people. Our dedication to shaping the leaders of tomorrow shines through our highly regarded graduate programme and ensures we stay ahead in a competitive market. From day one you will be on a stellar career trajectory with no limits to what you can achieve, and the programme empowers you to develop the essential skills needed to excel in the fast\-paced construction industry. Since launching in 2009, we have successfully trained 360\+ graduates, many of whom have forged long\-lasting careers with us. Our comprehensive eighteen\-month programme is tailored to meet real\-world demands, featuring courses led by both external and internal subject matter experts in a focused learning environment. Graduates on our programme can anticipate working alongside a diverse and supportive team, accessing state\-of\-the\-art training and masterclasses, and gaining international experience at the leading edge of industry. An important aspect of the development programme is the completion of a group research project. Graduates are split into small groups of four or five and assigned a strategic business project to research and present their findings to Senior Management. All topics are chosen by Senior Management and are relevant to Mercury's business. **Key Responsibilities Of The Role** * Co\-ordinating with site teams * Attend daily/ weekly meetings as required * Prepare reports/presentations as required * Assisting Intermediate and Senior Engineers * Ensure design complies with project specifications * Prepare technical submittals in accordance with project specifications and procurement schedule * Ensure materials / plant ordered are approved and in compliance with specification. * Work with BIM to develop construction plan and work packaging **Essential** **Selection Criteria / Person Specification:** * Ideally 2:1 Honours Degree / Expected 2:1 * Level 8 or relevant bachelor’s degree * Good communication skills * Strong interpersonal and organisational skills * Adaptable and solutions driven * Able to work in a team environment * Pride in the quality and presentation of work * Good organization \& co\-ordination skills * Flexible to travel * Enthusiastic and energetic **Desirable** * Experience in the Construction Industry or Trade Background ***Mercury is an equal opportunities employer*** Apply Now

title: Graduate Program
description: **Launch Your Global Career After Graduation** **Teach English in China \| Full\-Time \| Entry\-Level Friendly** Just graduated (or graduating soon) and not ready for a desk job? title: Graduate Sales & Application Engineer
description: **Launch Your Engineering Career with a Global Industry Leader** Are you a recent engineering graduate looking to combine technical problem\-solving with a future career in commercial engineering and sales? SPX Cooling Technologies is offering an exciting opportunity for a Graduate Sales \& Applications Engineer to join our Cooling Towers \& Accessories team in Ireland. This newly created role is designed to develop the next generation of technical and commercial talent within our business. You will gain hands\-on experience working on real HVAC and process cooling projects, learning how to review technical specifications, support quotations, and help deliver engineered solutions for customers across multiple industries. This is an ideal opportunity for someone who enjoys engineering detail, wants exposure to commercial projects, and is eager to develop toward a future customer\-facing engineering or sales role. **Why Join SPX Cooling Technologies?** * Join a global leader in cooling tower and thermal management solutions * Structured graduate development and mentoring from experienced engineers * Gain exposure to HVAC, process cooling, and industrial applications * Learn both the technical and commercial sides of engineering projects * Clear long\-term progression into applications engineering, technical sales, and leadership roles * Supportive team environment with ongoing training and development **The Role** As a Graduate Sales \& Applications Engineer, you will support the technical and commercial teams by assisting with product selection, proposal preparation, and engineering analysis. You will work closely with experienced colleagues to learn SPX systems, engineering tools, and customer requirements while developing your technical confidence and commercial awareness. **Key Responsibilities** **Technical Support** * Assist with reviewing RFQs, specifications, and technical drawings * Learn to use SPX selection software (QTC) to configure suitable solutions * Support the preparation of technical proposals and submittal documents * Help ensure product selections meet customer and project requirements * Work with internal engineering and sales teams on project coordination **Commercial \& Sales Exposure** * Support quotation and proposal development * Attend customer meetings and presentations alongside senior team members * Develop understanding of pricing, project delivery, and market competitors * Assist with maintaining project pipeline and sales data * Build commercial awareness within a technical engineering environment **What We’re Looking For** * Degree in Mechanical Engineering, Energy Engineering, Building Services Engineering, or similar * Strong interest in HVAC, cooling systems, thermodynamics, or industrial engineering * Good analytical and problem\-solving skills * Ability to interpret technical information and engineering drawings * Strong communication skills and willingness to learn * Motivated individual looking to develop a long\-term engineering career **Development \& Career Progression** This role has been designed as a long\-term development opportunity. You will receive structured support and mentoring with a clear pathway into: * Applications Engineering * Technical Sales * Key Account Management * Commercial Leadership If you are looking for a graduate engineering role that offers both technical depth and future commercial progression, we would love to hear from you.

title: Graduate Trainee, Strategic Communications and Public Affairs
description: Ankura is a team of excellence founded on innovation and growth. This position supports the Red Flag Global Practice which is part of the Ankura Consulting Group **Practice Overview** *Red Flag* is a world\-leading strategic communications and campaigns agency that has developed, driven and won campaigns for some of the world’s leading companies, trade associations and coalitions. We are now part of Ankura, the world\-leading expert services and advisory firm, who have more than 2,000 staff across 35 offices around the globe. **The Position** Red Flag operates a very popular and successful graduate training programme in its Dublin office which offers graduates with degrees in public relations or other relevant fields the opportunity to work alongside our experienced team at Red Flag where they will gain hands\-on experience developing their skills across strategic consulting, political and regulatory advice, reputation management, crisis communications and campaigning. Red Flag believes strongly in promoting from within, so graduates who excel during their six month graduate training placement may be offered a full\-time position when the programme ends. **Requirements** * Minimum of 2\.1 postgraduate qualification in public relations or relevant field * Strong writing skills (ability to write press releases, communications plans, speaking points, social media content, etc.); ability to understand and write clearly about technical subjects. * High level of comfort communicating with clients and journalists. * Media relations experience and media skills/contacts preferred. * Interest in digital communications and experience working with social media tools are desirable. * Excellent communication and organisational skills as well as the ability to prioritise and multi\-task in a creative, challenging and fast\-paced work environment. * Ability to work well with others in a team environment. * Proactive approach and willingness to take the initiative. Ankura is an Affirmative Action and Equal Opportunity Employer. title: HFS Fund Accounting Associate
description: **JOB DESCRIPTION** **Job Summary:** The Fund Accounting Associate is a member of the Fund Accounting team who will be primarily responsible for overseeing the NAV production process and ensuring accurate and timely calculation of the NAV in line with procedures. They will be a point of contact for collaborating across functions. The Fund Accounting Associate will work with team members and team managers to ensure all issues are resolved in a satisfactory manner. The Fund Accounting Associate will also be required to work closely with the wider AIS line of business (Investor Relations, Compliance, Internal Audit, Private Equity and Real Estate) in addition to 2nd line of defence partners. **Job Responsibilities:** * Responsible for the timely accurate calculation of the NAV. * Ability to investigate \& validate NAV movement. * Assist the team to ensure production and review of the daily/weekly/ad hoc/monthly NAV production. * Adhere to and ensure adherence to a defined control environment. * Identification of risks and implementation of remediation on the same. * Contribute to ownership and responsibility of the end to end NAV Operating model. * Manage resource planning of the team while partnering with wider teams. * Assist in Client Due Diligence visits along with Internal and External audit reviews. * Be a key driver and unwavering advocate to deliver best in class services internally and externally * Build strong relationships with stakeholders, clients, third parties, as well as with other business groups across JPMorgan * Understanding of Alternative investment fund types across multiple jurisdictions * Adhere to JPMorgan's established processes and procedures * Strive to modify / iterate processes and flows to continually improve accuracy, reduce redundancy, and mitigate risk **Qualifications** * Experience in a Fund Accounting Team at this level * Strong analytical and problem solving skills * In\-depth knowledge of the treatment of complex financial instruments in the NAV and NAV drivers. * Strong Client management skills and ability to build key relationships. * Team player, professional and proactive approach to work, positive attitude with an ability to communicate across multiple sites * Very strong communication skills both oral and written * Strong organizational skills with a drive for impeccable quality * Ability to work with a high level of precision in a fast paced, deadline driven environment * Ability to work independently with strong follow through **Required qualifications, capabilities, and skills** * Bachelors degree required (business discipline preferred) * Significant experience in the Financial Services industry, preferably with prior experience in alternative funds * **Client Service**: Anticipate and identify customer needs; take action to meet customer needs; develop effective working relationships with internal and external customers; continually search for ways to increase customer satisfaction. * **Risk Aversion**: Demonstrate a keen eye for details; look for and mitigate inherent and residual risks. * **Adaptability**: Confidently handle day\-to\-day work challenges; willing and able to adjust to multiple demands, shifting priorities, ambiguity, and rapid change; shows resilience in the face of constraints, frustrations, or adversity. * **High Aptitude**: Able to quickly understand technical, procedural and functional information to solve problems and deliver information to clients and investors. * **Communicate Effectively**: Demonstrate and convey an understanding of the comments and concerns of others; listen well in a group * include others in problem‑solving efforts when appropriate * **Foster Teamwork:** Contribute to and Build effective teams committed to organizational goals; fosters collaboration among team members title: HR Business Partner (Band 6)
description: Vacancy Number: 28619 Category: Human Resources Job Type: Full\-time **Position description** The capability of our people is core to ESB’s success. HR works in partnership with the business to ensure we can attract, develop, motivate, and retain the talent required to deliver our Net Zero strategy. As a key member of the Generation Trading HR Team, the HR Business Partner will positively impact colleagues and leaders in the organisation whilst ensuring HR processes and programmes are implemented in a manner that adds value to the business. HR Business Partners advise, coach and support management teams on people\-related initiatives and matters. At ESB, you’ll join a purpose‑driven organisation committed to innovation, sustainability and continuous development. This role offers the chance to influence meaningful change and grow your HR career in a supportive, collaborative environment. **Key Responsibilities** * **Business Partnering \& Stakeholder Management:** Build trusted relationships across the business to understand business priorities, workforce needs and people related challenges. Work collaboratively with HR Centres of Excellence and People Operations to deliver people initiatives and support continuous improvement. * **People Strategy, Planning \& Delivery:** Develop HR plans, targets \& KPIs with business line managers. Use insights from the analysis of people related data and leading external practices to influence and inform business decisions. Take lead responsibility for key strategic HR areas on behalf of the HR Team for GT, e.g. succession. * **Talent \& Capability:** Identify the resourcing and capability needs of the business through succession and business planning processes. Work with Talent Acquisition (TA) to meet needs of the business. * **Employee Relations:** Coach people managers on Employee Relations issues in accordance with ESB Policies and Procedures. Ensure adherence to HR compliance and risk management frameworks * **Change \& Culture Enablement:** Support organisational change initiatives/projects and facilitate a high performing organisational culture through effective people management and development. Support the implementation of Group\-wide HR initiatives within GT. * As an experienced member of team, support knowledge\-sharing within the team. Contribute to a positive, supportive team environment, by living our ESB values \& behaviours. **Experience and Qualifications** **Essential** * 7 years’ relevant work experience. * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills. * Ability to build and develop key relationships with managers and other stakeholders. * Ability to work cross functionally with colleagues on a variety of projects. * Proven analytical, judgement and decision\-making skills * Attention to detail, with strong analytical and problem\-solving skills. **Desirable** * Background in HR and knowledge of HR Practice, Policies and Procedures * An understanding of relevant legislation and procedures * Membership of CIPD **Location** This position will be based in an ESB Head Office Dublin, however travel to locations such as Moneypoint and other Asset locations will be a regular feature of the role. Smart Working at ESB is designed to make ‘in\-person’ time together as purposeful as possible while also enabling the effective use of digital tools and hybrid arrangements to preserve our commitment to flexibility. The successful candidate may work flexibly within the jurisdiction where they have been employed, with attendance at the workplace for in\-person collaboration **at least two days per week**. All Smart Working arrangements are subject to regular review based on the operational requirements of the role, the team, and the business. **Reporting To** Senior HR Business Partner **Why Work with Us?** * Opportunity to lead Ireland’s transition to net zero carbon future * Career development through mentoring and training * Corporate Social Responsibility Opportunities * Sports and Social Clubs * Networking opportunities * Credit Union * Generous Pension * Access to staff well\-being programmes * Generous parental leave entitlements * Strong values\-based and inclusive culture * Strong commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion * Great team environment working to our Core Values: Courageous, Caring, Driven and Trusted **Salary** €71,976 \- €84,677 per annum **Closing Date** 25th June 2026 **Interview** Candidates who are shortlisted for interview will complete a Behavioural Interview based on each of the following Behaviours, which underpin how we work at ESB and are critical to delivering our strategy: * Accountable and Ethical * Collaborative * Growing and Adaptive * Proactive * Respectful and Supportive More information available on Our People Promise **Notes** Your application will be held in reserve for 6 months should you be suitable for the role. The successful candidate will be required to evidence their right to work in the Republic of Ireland prior to commencement of employment. While initial appointment will be to this position, rotation within Generation and Trading may be a feature of the role. **Diversity, Equity and Inclusion Statement** ESB is committed to being an equal opportunities employer. We welcome applications from all sections of society and ensure that no one is discriminated against on the grounds of race, religion or belief, ethnicity or nationality, disability, age, citizenship, marital status, domestic or civil partnership status, sexual orientation or gender identity, or any other basis as protected by law. Applicants who anticipate requiring assistance or reasonable accommodations for any part of the application or interview process may contact, in confidence, recruitment@esb.ie. **About ESB** Join us in our mission to achieve a net\-zero electricity system by 2040\. ESB is Ireland’s leading energy utility, with activities spanning electricity generation, transmission and distribution, energy supply, energy services and international consultancy. Operating in Ireland, Northern Ireland and Great Britain, we invest over €1 billion each year to deliver a new energy future based on reliable, affordable zero\-carbon electricity.

title: HSE Advisor
description: **Building a sustainable tomorrow** Working at BAM means delivering projects that make a difference. From the major infrastructure that keeps society moving, to the social housing, schools, and community spaces that shape daily life. We don’t just talk about social value. We embed it into every build. We are recruiting a **Project Health,Safety \& Environmental Advisor** to join our Carlow based project. The role of the Project HSE Advisor is to guide, support and advise Project Managers and other relevant personnel on the setting up, implementation and continuous maintenance of HSE systems and standards on site in accordance with BAM Safety and Environmental Management Systems. **Making Possible** * Promote safe working practices and ensure the implementation of company HSE policy and procedures on site. * Carry out regular site HSE inspections and report back findings to relevant personal. * Ensure that the project has been provided with the relevant HSE documentation. * Ensure that HSE project plans meet company standards. * Ensure that HSE issues are discussed at project review meetings. * Keep and maintain clear records to comply with company internal and external audit standards. * Deliver HSE training where appropriate and as agreed with the Project Manager. * Keep up\-to\-date on Health, Safety and Environmental legislation. * Communicate Demonstrate and Promote BAM HSE ethos, values and concepts. * Become a Role Model for Safe Behaviour. * Carry out other relevant duties as required from time to time. * Liaising with the relevant legal authorities (i.e. H.S.A, EPA etc.) * Knowledge of all legal requirements especially in regard to the PSCS role and duties. * Ensure all accidents and dangerous incidents are thoroughly investigated and reports provided to the HSE \& * Sustainability Manager detailing causes, conclusions and recommendations to avoid recurrence. * Provide the HSE Department with relevant HSE information on a quarterly basis. **What’s in it for you?** Opportunities! Opportunities to work with a great team on some of Irelands largest, dynamic and most exciting construction and civil engineering projects. Opportunity to develop your skills to the highest industry standards at one of the country's largest construction firms. We are committed to our employees and offer strong competitive salaries along with benefits such as: * 8% pension contribution. * Private Health Insurance. * Dental Plan. * 26 days annual leave, incremental with years service. * 2 Wellbeing days annually. * Paid maternity \& paternity leave. * Learning \& Development opportunities \- internal progression is encouraged and fostered. * Company vehicles (role dependent). * .....and many more great perks. **What do you bring to the role?** * Level 7 qualification in Health and Safety. * 3 years successful postgraduate experience in Project HSE Management. * Thorough knowledge of ISO: 45001 and ISO: 14001 standards. * Working towards graduate membership of IOSH. * Ability to deliver internal Health and Safety management courses. * Ability to advise on HSE issues on a day to day basis on site. * Ability to conduct standard accident/incident investigations. * Ability to write HSE site reports. * Qualification in Environmental Management is desirable. **About BAM** Building a sustainable tomorrow. That’s our mission and our promise at BAM. It’s how we engineer vital infrastructure and construct high\-quality buildings as one of the largest construction companies in Europe. We strive to create an environment where everybody feels welcome and valued. We’re on an exciting journey to employ the best talent to join us regardless of social background, race, colour, religion, national or ethnic origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability or other characteristics. **The application process** BAM is committed to ensuring a fully inclusive recruitment and onboarding process, so if at any time you feel we need to do something to make it more accessible to you, do not hesitate to let us know. Click the link to apply and Join us in making possible \#ie1 * Please note, CV's will not be accepted directly by email.

title: Healthcare Assistant
description: **Company Description** Liberty Living Homecare is dedicated to providing highly personal homecare that reflects each person’s individual needs and preferences. The organization believes that care should look different for everyone, enabling families to focus on quality time together while care professionals manage day\-to\-day support. Services are designed to promote comfort, independence, and peace of mind for people receiving care in their own homes. With expert clinical oversight, Liberty Living Homecare maintains high standards of safety, dignity, and compassion in every interaction. Families are offered ongoing reassurance that their loved ones are in safe, skilled, and caring hands. **Role Description** This is a full\-time, on\-site Healthcare Assistant role based in Dublin. The Healthcare Assistant will provide person\-centered care and support with daily living activities such as personal hygiene, mobility, meal preparation, and companionship in clients’ homes or residential settings. Responsibilities include observing and reporting changes in clients’ physical or emotional condition, supporting safe administration of medicines under appropriate guidance, and following individual care plans. The role involves maintaining a clean, safe environment, adhering to infection prevention and control procedures, and complying with all health and safety standards. The Healthcare Assistant will work collaboratively with nurses, clinical leads, and other team members, maintaining clear documentation and communicating respectfully with clients and their families. **Qualifications** * Strong patient care skills, with experience supporting personal care, mobility, and daily living activities in a respectful, person\-centered manner. * Proficiency in safe manual handling techniques and an understanding of best practices for moving and assisting individuals. * Knowledge of basic medicine support procedures, including following care plans and working under appropriate clinical guidance. * Solid understanding of health \& safety, infection prevention and control, and risk awareness in home and residential care environments. * Excellent interpersonal skills, including clear communication, empathy, and the ability to build trust with clients, families, and colleagues. * Relevant healthcare or social care qualification (e.g., QQI/FETAC Level 5 or equivalent) or willingness to obtain it within an agreed timeframe. * Previous experience in homecare, community care, or a healthcare setting is an advantage. * Reliable, organized, and able to work flexibly, including evenings or weekends as required.

The ideal candidate brings strong operational rigor, deep identity expertise, and experience working within structured IT service management environments. You are comfortable balancing reactive operational support with proactive platform improvement. This is your opportunity to join a world\-class team and make a significant impact! **Responsibilities** Microsoft 365 \& Identity Operations * Manage daily administration and operational support for Microsoft 365 and Entra ID services. * Triage and resolve incidents and service requests across identity, messaging, collaboration, and access platforms. * Perform root cause analysis and implement long\-term remediation to reduce recurring issues and technical debt. * Manage identity lifecycle processes, RBAC, privileged role governance, Conditional Access, MFA, and authentication policies. * Troubleshoot authentication and access issues using Entra ID audit and sign\-in logs. * Coordinate Exchange Online, including mail flow, transport rules, mailbox management, permissions, and email security configurations such as SPF, DKIM, DMARC, and anti\-spam/phishing controls. * Operate within an enterprise ITSM framework (e.g., ServiceNow or Jira Service Management), managing tickets in alignment with SLAs and operational benchmarks. * Develop dashboards and reporting to track ticket trends, SLA adherence, and overall platform health. * Maintain runbooks and knowledge documentation while driving continuous service improvement and automation. Automation, Integrations \& Microsoft Graph * Develop and sustain PowerShell automation for Microsoft 365 and identity management. * Register and manage Entra ID applications, including service principals, API permissions, and consent models. * Implement secure Microsoft Graph integrations using delegated and application permissions, ensuring least\-privilege access and accurate certificate and secret management procedures. **Qualifications:** * 5\+ years of hands\-on experience coordinating Microsoft 365 and identity platforms in an enterprise environment. * Solid operational knowledge of Microsoft Entra ID and Exchange Online. * Experience supporting enterprise IT service operations using ITSM platforms (e.g., ServiceNow, Jira Service Desk), with understanding of SLAs, incident management, and reporting. * Direct involvement in handling Entra ID application registrations and Microsoft Graph integrations, including delegated as opposed to application permission models. * Proficient in PowerShell scripting for managing Microsoft 365 environments. **Preferred:** * Experience working with Microsoft Intune and its integration with Conditional Access. * Experience working with Microsoft 365 migration solutions like ShareGate, AvePoint, or Quest. * Familiarity with Power Platform governance and security controls. * Experience supporting large\-scale or global enterprise environments. * Familiarity with Microsoft 365 security and compliance tools (DLP, eDiscovery). * Experience supporting tenant\-to\-tenant migrations across Exchange, SharePoint, OneDrive, and Teams, including post\-migration validation, permissions remediation, integration reconfiguration, and broader Microsoft platform modernization efforts. description: Global Operations Interaction Evaluator Quality Analyst, German Speaker \- Trust \& Safety Location : Dublin Employment Type : Regular Job Code : A112723B Responsibilities About the team The Search and Interaction Safety Operations team will assume a critical role in ensuring the seamless operation of TikTok's search and interaction feature, implementing operational processes, and overseeing day\-to\-day activities. By proactively addressing safety concerns and enhancing operational efficiency, the team will significantly contribute to TikTok's mission of providing a safe, smooth, and enjoyable search experience for users worldwide. **Responsibilities:** * Develop and implement quality control processes to ensure adherence to established guidelines and standards. * Monitor the quality of content review for complex and high\-quality content and ensure credible quality result output. * Identify opportunities for process improvement that will enhance the review of complex content. * Provide feedback to team members about quality performance outcomes, analysis, and interpretation of data. * Develop and implement training for team members to enhance the quality of their work output. * Work closely with cross\-functional teams to establish quality improvement goals, targets, and priorities. * Create and execute projects aimed at enhancing the priority and quality of content review. * Communicate outlined expectations and provide regular progress updates to stakeholders and senior management. * Participate in other ad\-hoc projects as assigned by the team lead. Job Information Trust \& Safety Content that this role interacts with includes images, video, and text related to every\-day life, but it can also include (but is not limited to) bullying; hate speech; child safety; depictions of harm to self and others, and harm to animals. Hence, it is possible that this role will be exposed to harmful content on a daily basis. ​ ​ TikTok recognises that keeping our platform safe for the TikTok communities is no ordinary job which can be both rewarding and psychologically demanding and emotionally taxing for some. This is why we are sharing the potential hazards, risks and implications in this unique line of work from the start, so our candidates are well informed before joining. ​ ​ We are committed to the wellbeing of all our employees and promise to provide comprehensive and evidence\-based programs, to promote and support physical and mental wellbeing throughout each employee's journey with us. We believe that wellbeing is a relationship and that everyone has a part to play, so we work in collaboration and consultation with our employees and across our functions in order to ensure a truly person\-centred, innovative and integrated approach. ​ About TikTok TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo. ​ Why Join Us Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day. ​ We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us. ​ ​ Diversity \& Inclusion ​ TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

or areas for improvement on maintenance processes * Suggest changes to PM frequency, task content \& work instructions. **Cross\-Team Communication \& Escalation** * Attend and contribute to daily and Systems Used:** * CMMS (EAMS) * MS Excel / Planning Dashboards * Microsoft Teams / Outlook / SharePoint * Technical drawings, job plan libraries, P\&IDs **Minimum Qualifications and Experience:** * title: Junior Software Engineer, Platform

Best Workplace awardee. **About The Role** We are seeking an exceptional **Junior Engineer** to join our Platform Group, where you will architect and develop shared infrastructure components that power Semperis's enterprise security and identity products. technical standards * Drive technical initiatives that improve developer productivity, system observability, and platform resilience * Contribute to architectural decisions and establish best practices for backend development across the organization * Analyze system performance and data patterns to identify optimization opportunities and inform future platform investments What You Bring features and performance optimization * Production experience with Kubernetes and container orchestration in cloud environments (Azure/AWS/GCP preferred) * Experience developing shared libraries, Knowledge of ElasticSearch or other distributed search/analytics platforms * Open\-source contributions or experience maintaining shared component libraries * Experience with observability tools (Prometheus, Grafana, distributed tracing) * Background in API design and governance for platform services * Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and infrastructure\-as\-code practices **Why Join title: Legal Counsel - Corporate
description: At Perrigo, we are driven by our mission to ***Makes Lives Better Through Trusted Health and Wellness Solutions, Accessible to All***. We are proud to be a Top 10 player in the European Consumer Self\-Care market and the largest U.S. store brand provider of over the counter and infant formula. Dedicated to providing ***The Best Self\-Care for Everyone***, we are the people behind the brands you trust. We are Opill®, Compeed®, Solpadeine®, NiQuitin®, and many more. **We Are Perrigo**. We are committed to enhancing the wellbeing of our colleagues and consumers alike. We pride ourselves on fostering an inclusive, collaborative culture where each person can experience a sense of belonging. Join us on our One Perrigo journey as we evolve to win in self\-care. **Description Overview** ------------------------ This is an exciting opportunity to apply your legal expertise to Perrigo’s Corporate Legal team, which operates at the intersection of law and business and provides strategic legal counsel across areas such as Business Development, Corporate Governance, Corporate Finance, Tax, Treasury, and Investor Relations. As **Legal Counsel – Corporate**, you will play a crucial role in supporting the Deputy General Counsel across a broad range of high‑impact corporate matters, with **a primary focus on leading and supporting M\&A and strategic transactions**, corporate governance for a **public company headquartered in Ireland**, and complex global corporate operations. The selected candidate will be based at our Global Headquarters in **Dublin, Ireland**. While Dublin is strongly preferred, we will also consider exceptional candidates for our **London, England** office. A hybrid working schedule applies in either location. **Scope of the Role** --------------------- * M\&A: Lead and support Perrigo’s global M\&A and strategic transaction activity, including: + Managing and coordinating cross‑functional legal workstreams on acquisitions, divestitures, strategic partnerships, and other transformative transactions. + Conducting and overseeing due diligence; drafting, reviewing, and negotiating transaction agreements; and supporting carve‑out, integration planning, restructuring, and related corporate approvals. + Preparing and coordinating antitrust/competition and other regulatory filings across multiple jurisdictions. + Advising senior leadership on transaction risks, opportunities, and structuring considerations in a dynamic multinational environment. * Board \& Corporate Governance: Together with a Legal Analyst, play a critical role in supporting Perrigo’s Board of Directors and governance framework for a NYSE‑listed, Irish‑domiciled company, including: + Preparing and managing materials for quarterly Board and Committee meetings, including agendas, resolutions, briefing memos, and minutes. + Partnering with the Deputy General Counsel to maintain, update, and enhance global governance policies and governance best practices across U.S. and Irish legal frameworks. + Supporting the Annual General Meeting (AGM), including planning, drafting AGM materials, managing shareholder and regulatory requirements in Ireland, and ensuring compliance with Irish and U.S. governance norms. + Leading quarterly governance updates, monitoring regulatory and stewardship‑related developments (e.g. ISS, Glass Lewis, Irish law, NYSE), and presenting trends to senior leadership. + Developing and delivering training modules and education materials for Directors, including Ireland\-specific governance dynamics and cross‑jurisdictional fiduciary duties. * Subsidiary Entity Management: Together with the Corporate Governance Analyst, serve as a senior legal advisor on Perrigo’s global corporate matters, including: + Overseeing global entity management, board/corporate secretarial functions, and corporate recordkeeping across Europe, North America, and other regions. + Supporting subsidiary governance enhancements and internal corporate simplification efforts. * Corporate Finance, Disclosure and Securities Regulation: Provide targeted, strategic support on U.S. securities law matters, including: + Assisting with periodic reporting and disclosure considerations. + Reviewing press releases, earnings materials, and investor communications for legal and risk considerations. + Advising on insider trading, Section 16, and corporate disclosure controls as needed. * Tax \& Treasury: Provide targeted, strategic support on tax \& treasury matters, including financing activities; M\&A\-related tax structuring; and domestic and international tax matters. * General Corporate Matters: Provide legal support on a broad range of general corporate matters, including entity management, corporate compliance, and other day\-to\-day corporate legal issues impacting the company’s operations. Serve as a cross functional strategic partner to Finance, Tax, and Global Business Development. **Experience Required** ----------------------- * A qualified and licensed attorney; admission to practice law in Ireland or England. * Typically 6\-10\+ years of legal experience working with international publicly traded companies, preferably with a combination of law firm and in\-house experience. * The ideal candidate will have a proven track record of law firm M\&A experience and adjacent corporate practice areas. * Demonstrated ability to manage senior executive relations and ability to collaborate and build relations with stakeholders across functional and organizational lines. * Strong understanding of relevant laws and regulations governing the industry, combined with an ability to explain complex legal concepts in simple, actionable terms to stakeholders located in different jurisdictions. * Strong strategic thinking and business acumen, with the ability to balance legal considerations with business objectives. * Exceptional leadership, interpersonal and communication skills, including well\-developed interpersonal skills and strong written communication skills. * Discretion \& Integrity – the ability to handle sensitive information with the highest level of confidentiality and ethics. * Project management skills – the ability to manage multiple legal matters and deadlines. Familiarity with legal tech tools is also a bonus. **Benefits** We believe our people are our greatest asset. Alongside competitive compensation, we offer benefits tailored to supporting you and your family, as well as career development opportunities to ensure you feel valued and supported, both professionally and personally. Find out more about **Total Rewards** at Perrigo. **Hybrid Working Approach** We love our offices and the setting they provide for in\-person collaboration and celebration. But we also appreciate the opportunity to work remotely can energise you too, so we promote flexibility with the ability to work two days a week from home in many roles. We are proud to be included in the Forbes list of "America's Best Employers by State 2024". Find out more **here** **Applicants please note:** To apply to this position please click the APPLY button at the bottom of the application. (The SAVE button will only save your profile information but not submit an application for this open position.) Thank you. *We are an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, status as a protected veteran, or other characteristics protected by law. \#weareperrigo*

title: Loss Prevention Investigator
description: **Your team** The Retail Loss Prevention team supports our shop\-based colleagues across the UK and Ireland, mitigating actual and potential risks to the business, protecting our colleagues and customers, investigating fraud and minimising financial loss. Supporting more than 550 shops across the UK and Ireland the team works closely with Retail Operations, Compliance, Employee Relations, external agencies and third\-party suppliers. The team plays a vital role in reducing financial loss, protecting margin, supporting regulatory standards and responding to the day\-to\-day challenges faced across a lively retail betting estate. This role follows a hybrid working approach, combining: * Working from home * Field\-based shop visits * A minimum of two days per week either in the office or completing field activity * Occasional planned travel to Dublin, Leeds or London * Occasional evening or weekend phone support when required **What you’ll do** * Build trusted, collaborative relationships with Retail District Operations Managers, shop teams and key stakeholders to mitigate risk, loss prevention and review of margin as well as to provide support * Conduct investigations into all alleged acts of misconduct or non\-compliance with regulatory standards whilst always maintain appropriate ER practices. * Conduct shop\-based Compliance \& Loss Prevention audits during shop visits to identify and failings and drive continuous improvement * Liaise with the Gardai around investigating theft, robberies and criminal behaviour ensuring evidence packs are produced and submitted to the required standards. * You will also play a pivotal role in preparing the Irish retail estate for the evolving regulatory landscape following the establishment of the Gambling Regulatory Authority of Ireland (GRAI). **How you’ll do it** * Experience or in\-depth knowledge of the role of a retail loss prevention investigator, ideally across a multi\-site customer facing operation. * Ability to collate, assess and present complex data in a way which identifies key trends and exceptions * Strong decision making and prioritisation skills * Excellent written and verbal communication skills. * Experience working with third party suppliers and external agencies **What’s on offer.** * Unlimited Holiday * Annual Bonus * Car allowance * £/€1,000 learning fund * €250 Wellbeing fund * Pension contribution scheme * Private healthcare * Hybrid Working – Minimum 2 days in Office of field based per week. * Access to thousands of Udemy courses * Invest via the Company Sharesave Scheme * 16 Hours paid volunteering each year. **About PaddyPower** ***Work that makes play jealous*** Paddy Power has been breaking and re\-writing the rules since 1988, from on\-track bookmakers to cheeky upstart mischief\-makers, to part of the largest betting and gaming technology company in the world. Although we’ve grown to the betting behemoth we are today, we’ve not forgotten our roots. We continue to offer great value and entertainment to our customers and keep them at the heart of what we do. Our culture of hard work, boldness and an innovative spirit got us to where we are and we take our work seriously, but not ourselves! We win by having the best people working well together. Don’t pigeon\-hole us either – our company is a mix of sports nuts, gaming innovators, tech geniuses, perceptive people\-people, inspiring leaders… and a very busy legal team. We’ve got a growing squad of specialists, and we make the work fun. Sound good? Maybe there’s a spot on the team for you! Plus, PaddyPower is part of the Flutter UK \& Ireland family – a collection of big\-hitting entertainment brands such as Betfair, Sky Betting \& Gaming and Tombola. **About Flutter UK \& Ireland** We are Flutter UK \& Ireland, part of the Global FTSE 100 Flutter Entertainment. Our division operates four of the most popular and trusted brands in the market: Sky Betting and Gaming, Tombola, Betfair and Paddy Power. Together the Flutter UKI brands offer market leading entertainment to millions of customers every single week! Over the last couple of decades, they have all ridden the wave of digital betting with industry firsts like ‘cash\-out’, ‘Request a Bet’, betting exchanges and free to play games like Soccer Saturday Super 6\. **Working Here** Do you want to work somewhere extraordinary? From the people you spend your days with, to the ground\-breaking projects, no two days will be the same. Our 7,000\+ colleagues come together to form an expert, encouraging community across technology, product, commercial, data, infrastructure, marketing, and a myriad of subject areas. And the Values that guide us? Here they are: **Customer First, Always \| Change The Game \| Win Together \| Free To Be Me** We’re proud of our 4 Values, they reflect what matters most to our people and will guide the decisions we make, represent what we believe in and support how we do things at Flutter UK \& Ireland. Find out more about how we co\-created our Values with over 10,000\+ insights from our people! **We’re working to be an inclusive employer, and we encourage people from all backgrounds, ways of thinking and working to apply. Everyone brings different perspectives and experiences; you don't have to meet all the requirements listed to apply for this role.**

title: Loss Prevention Officer- Blanchardstown
description: **Job Description** **SHIFTS YOU ARE APPLYING FOR : Rotational Shifts** **The Role:** Working within our Loss Prevention team at NEXT within our Blanchardstown store you’ll effectively support the provision of a safe and secure working and shopping environment, providing a uniformed presence to protect the company assets and to minimise loss. **What You'll Take On:** * Ensure our stores feel safe and secure places to work and shop. * Work closely with store teams and build strong relationships with local police. * Identify any high\-risk areas and act as a strong deterrent on the shop floor. * Engage with store management to ensure company LP standards within stores are adhered to at all times. * Action any other duties as requested by the Regional LP Manager including out of hours surveillance, LP auditing and additional support at other stores and attend department meetings. **What You'll Bring:** * A valid PSA licence * Previous security experience within a retail environment is preferred. * Flexibility and willingness to work across different stores and opening times. * Able to travel on a day to day basis to cover a cluster of stores within a reasonable travelling distance from your home address. * A commitment to keeping our store teams and customers safe * Well organised with the ability to work well in a fast and busy store. * Hardworking, self motivated and always ready to use your initiative. * A confident communicator who can build great relationships with store teams, police and customers. * Fully flexible to work 5 out of 7 days including weekends and evenings. * Comfortable with using IT systems/computers is preferred but not essential. **About Us** You know Next, but did you know we’re a FTSE\-100 retail company employing over 44,000 people across the Next group. title: Mechanical Shift Technician
description: **Background:** Veolia is Ireland’s leading environmental services company. We provide a comprehensive range of energy, waste and water solutions and are dedicated to carbon reduction, protecting the environment and building the circular economy. We offer all employees a benefits package as standard which includes full VHI cover, pension and education assistance. When you see the world as we do, you see the chance to help the world take better care of its resources, and help it become a better place for everyone. It's why we're looking for someone who's just as committed as we are, to push for genuine change and bring our ambition of Ecological Transformation to life. We know that everyone here at Veolia can help us work alongside our communities, look after the environment, and contribute to our inclusive culture. **Mechanical Shift Technician** **Location: Blanchardstown** **Duration: Permanent** **Hours \- Shift** **(2 days, 2 nights, 4 days off)** **Overview of the role;** The Utilities Technician is responsible for the safe, compliant, and reliable operation and maintenance of clean utilities, black utilities, HVAC and refrigeration, fire alarm/suppression systems, wastewater treatment, and medium\-voltage (MV) electrical distribution that support GMP manufacturing and life safety. The role operates within controlled cleanroom and technical environments and requires adherence to cGMP, GDP, and EHS standards, delivering high availability of critical services on a rotating shift. **Day to Day of the role;** * Clean Utilities Operations and Maintenance: Operate, monitor, and maintain purified water (PW), water for injection (WFI), clean steam, clean compressed air, and clean gases. Execute sanitisation/heat cycles, support sampling, trend CQAs/CPPs, manage biofilm risk, and ensure system state of control for GMP use points. * Black Utilities Operations and Maintenance: Maintain boilers/steam distribution, condensate return, chillers, cooling towers, plant compressed air/vacuum, and heating hot water. Optimise energy efficiency and reliability, ensuring compliance with statutory requirements (e.g., legionella control, pressure systems). * HVAC \& Refrigeration: Operate and maintain AHUs serving cleanrooms and labs to meet temperature, RH, and differential pressure specifications. Perform filter changes, support HEPA integrity testing, and troubleshoot airflows. Maintain refrigeration assets (DX systems, packaged chillers, cold rooms/freezers), ensuring F\-gas compliance, leak checks, and performance optimisation. * Fire Alarm \& Suppression Systems: Support inspection, testing, and maintenance of fire detection (addressable, aspirating) and suppression interfaces (sprinkler, gas where applicable). Execute approved isolations, issue/track red tags, and complete functional restoration tests in line with EHS and insurance requirements. * WWTP/Effluent: Operate and maintain screens, equalisation, biological/chemical treatment, DAF/MBBR/IFAS (as applicable), clarifiers, sludge handling, and dosing systems. Monitor and trend key parameters (pH, DO, ORP, temperature, conductivity, flow), coordinate sampling, and ensure compliance with discharge licences and incident response procedures. * MV Electrical Support: Support safe operation of MV switchgear and transformers. Assist authorised persons with switching plans, LOTO, and permits; monitor power quality and resilience, including coordination with UPS and generators. Engage with ESB and vendors as directed by site electrical authority. * Control Systems \& Alarm Response: Monitor systems via BMS/SCADA (e.g., NQBMS/QBMS/MCS) and fire panels. Respond to alarms per PRCs/SOPs, perform initial troubleshooting, and promptly escalate GMP/critical events to protect product and operations. Document actions and shift handovers. * Preventive \& Reactive Maintenance: Execute PMs, CMs, and predictive tasks; coordinate vendor interventions; manage spare parts; and close work orders in the CMMS to GDP standards with accurate parts/time entries and attachments. * Permitting, Isolations \& Safety: Prepare RAMS and permits to work; perform mechanical/electrical isolations (LOTO) and fire system isolations; verify and redline P\&IDs/SLDs/C\&E as needed; conduct pre\- and post\-work checks and return\-to\-service verifications. * Compliance \& Documentation: Work to cGMP/GDP, Irish/EU statutory sop s sisa tem; prepare logs, deviations, change controls, and CAPAs; support internal/external audits and regulatory inspections. * Troubleshooting \& Root Cause: Apply structured fault\-finding across mechanical, electrical, and control domains; support RCAs and implement corrective and preventive actions that sustainably address failure modes. * Commissioning \& Projects: Support FAT/SAT, commissioning, and handover for utilities and vendor packaged equipment; review test protocols, verify I/O, and ensure asset data and documentation are complete. * Continuous Improvement: Identify and implement reliability and energy/water/steam efficiency improvements; contribute to SOP updates, standard work, and best practices; track and report KPIs (uptime, alarm recurrence, energy metrics).Working in Controlled/Clean Environments * Maintain cleanroom discipline, gowning, and behaviour appropriate to ISO 8–ISO 5 areas as required by task. * Ensure HVAC parameters, pressure cascades, and filtration states are preserved during maintenance; plan interventions to minimise contamination risk. * Apply contamination control strategies (tool control, point\-of\-use sanitisation, clean breaks, material/personnel flows) and follow microbiological/particulate control procedures. * Observe GDP for all entries (indelible, timely, attributable, legible), and ensure use\-point impact assessments are completed before/after interventions. **Requirements for the role;** * Minimum Education/Trade: Recognised Irish trade qualification (QQI Level 6 Craft) in Electrical, Mechanical, Refrigeration, or Instrumentation, or Relevant third\-level qualification (Level 7 or Level 8\) in Mechanical, Electrical, Building Services, Mechatronics, or related engineering discipline. * 2\+ years in pharma/biopharma or regulated manufacturing preferred, with hands\-on exposure to clean utilities and at least two of: black utilities, HVAC/refrigeration, fire systems, WWTP, MV. * Operation and maintenance of PW/WFI/clean steam and cleanroom HVAC. * BMS/SCADA operation, alarm management, trend analysis, and setpoint governance. * Mechanical/electrical troubleshooting; reading P\&IDs, SLDs, cause\-and\-effect, fire strategies. * LOTO, permits to work, RAMS; basic water/steam chemistry; balancing/commissioning (TAB) support. * CMMS proficiency; vendor coordination and documentation control. * Advantageous: Steam systems optimisation (BOAS/steam awareness), WWTP operator training, ATEX awareness, power quality basics, refrigeration diagnostics. * Safe Pass, Manual Handling, Confined Space, Working at Height/MEWP. * F\-gas Category I/II (for refrigeration work). * First Aid Responder/Fire Marshal. * Electrical authorisation up to MV (as applicable to site). * Safety \& Compliance Focus: Makes safe decisions, follows EHS and life\-safety procedures rigorously. * Problem Solving \& Ownership: Structured diagnostics, prioritises critical alarms, and sees issues through to closure. * Communication \& Documentation: Clear handovers, accurate GDP records, and effective stakeholder updates. * Collaboration: Works cross\-functionally with Manufacturing, QA, EHS, Facilities, and vendors. * Continuous Improvement Mindset: Data\-driven, proactive in reliability and energy performance. * Work across plant rooms, cleanrooms, rooftops, WWTP, and outdoors; use of PPE; occasional lifting; work at height or in confined spaces subject to training and permits. * Participation in site emergency response as assigned. **Please note:** If you are an EU/EEA national, you will be asked to show proof of right to work in Ireland. If you are a non\-EU/EEA national , you will require current and valid permission to work and reside in the Republic of Ireland. Information on legislation and guides to the procedures in relation to obtaining greencard permits ,work permits, spousal/dependant permits is available on the Department of Jobs, Enterprise and Innovation website.

Where agency agreements are in place, introductions are position specific. Please, no phone calls or emails. **Employee Status:** Regular**Relocation:** **VISA Sponsorship:** **Travel Requirements:** **Flexible Work Arrangements:** Not Applicable**Shift:** **Valid Driving License:** **Hazardous Material(s):** **Job Posting End Date:** 06/24/2026* **A job posting is effective until 11:59:59PM on the day** **BEFORE** **the listed job posting end date.
