You'll work directly with senior leadership, take ownership of meaningful responsibilities from day one, and gain exposure to the core functions of a regulated fintech business. **What You'll Do** * Support Programme Managers and customers with payment, card, onboarding, and account\-related queries. * Assist with AML, KYC/KYB, sanctions screening, and compliance case management. * Support transaction monitoring and prepare case files for MLRO review. * Help maintain regulatory records, audit trails, and compliance documentation. * Assist with settlement, funding, and transaction reconciliations. * Contribute to operational procedures, process improvements, and internal documentation. * Collaborate across teams and take ownership of projects as the company grows. **About You** * Recent graduate or up to 2 years' experience in Finance, Business, Economics, Law, or a related discipline. * Strong interest in fintech, payments, compliance, or financial crime prevention. * Excellent attention to detail and organisational skills. * Comfortable working with data, spreadsheets, and operational processes. * Strong written communication skills. * Self\-starter who thrives in a fast\-paced, entrepreneurial environment. **Why ArmadilloPay?** * Join at the ground floor of a regulated fintech. * Work directly with the COO, CFO, and MLRO. * Gain exposure across operations, compliance, and finance. * Build skills that can accelerate a career in payments, fintech, risk, or financial crime compliance. * Take real ownership and make a visible impact from day one. If you're looking for a role that offers responsibility, learning, and the opportunity to help build something meaningful, we'd love to hear from you. **Apply now and be part of ArmadilloPay's growth journey.**

title: Operations Assistant
description: **Introduction** Established in 1951, IOM is a Related Organization of the United Nations and the leading UN agency in the field of migration. Working closely with governmental, intergovernmental and non\-governmental partners, IOM promotes humane and orderly migration for the benefit of all. It saves lives and protects people on the move, drives solutions to displacement, and facilitates pathways for regular migration, while providing services and advice to governments and migrants. IOM is committed to fostering a respectful, inclusive and supportive workplace where all employees can thrive professionally and feel valued. By creating such an environment, IOM aims to better harness the full potential of migration and strengthen its support to people on the move. IOM invites candidates from diverse backgrounds to apply and provides reasonable accommodation throughout the recruitment process when required. Learn more about IOM’s workplace culture at IOM workplace culture \| International Organization for Migration Applications are welcome from internal and external candidates. For all IOM vacancies, applications from qualified and eligible first\-tier candidates are considered before those of qualified and eligible second\-tier candidates in the selection process. For the purpose of this vacancy, internal and internal\-equivalent candidates are considered as first\-tier candidates. **Organizational Context and Scope** Under the overall supervision of the Chief of Mission (CoM) in Ireland, under the guidance of Head of Unit, Operations and under the direct supervision of Operations Associate and, in collaboration with Operations Assistants and relevant units at Headquarters and the Administrative Centres, the successful candidate will be responsible and accountable for supervising all movement operations activities in Ireland, with the following duties and responsibilities: Responsibilities: Coordinate, schedule and book travel for individuals upon receipt of travel\-ready status and/or request in accordance with travel requirements, including but not limited to the distribution of Advance Booking Notifications (ABNs), updates, domestic flights, cancellations and departure notifications. Organize and complete all bookings in a timely manner and in accordance with the Handbook of IOM Tariffs (HIT) and standard operating procedures (SOPs) from the Division of Resettlement and Movement Management (RMM). Under the supervision of the Project Associate distribute travel information to internal and external stakeholders. Compile and analyse descriptive statistics, using I\-GATOR to capture costs and prepare travel loan paperwork as specified in SOPs and in accordance with host government’s procedures. Create movement data files, by ABN, for all individuals in accordance with SOPs and for IOM accountability. In accordance with local practices and RMM guidelines and standards, and in close coordination with supervisors, identify and assign escorts to accompany vulnerable individuals. In coordination with Project Associate, process exit permission paperwork and ICRC Travel Documents in accordance with established standards and the local guidelines. Conduct identity and document verification prior to the distribution of travel documentation to refugees, immigrants and migrants. Conduct pre\-departure counselling on pre\-embarkation procedures and special needs during travel (such as meals, medication, wheelchairs and medical conditions) as needed. Identify beneficiary vulnerabilities and coordinate appropriate action to ensure they are addressed. Provide regular feedback on work being accomplished to the Senior Project Associate and keep supervisors immediately informed of any issues that arise. Demonstrate an in\-depth understanding of relevant Movement Operations SOPs and Movements\-related systems and databases (including iGATOR, MiMOSA, SAR and Amadeus), as well as the ability to remain professional, impartial and unbiased during all interactions with migrants and colleagues per the IOM Code of Conduct and instruction on the Prevention of Sexual Exploitation and Abuse (PSEA). Maintain and ensure the confidentiality and integrity of all relevant paperwork in line with standards of conduct and data protection rules. Alert Project Associate or management of any non\-compliance to SOPs or codes of conduct by IOM staff members or partners. * Perform such other duties as may be assigned. Qualifications: Required Qualifications and Experience Education Five years of relevant working experience with secondary education; Three years of relevant working experience with Bachelor’s degree in Social Studies, Political Science, International Relations, Psychology or related fields. Accredited Universities are those listed in the UNESCO World Higher Education Database. Experience Prior Movement Operations, transportation\-related and/or management experience a strong advantage. Prior work experience with international humanitarian organizations, non\-government or government institutions/organization in a multi\-cultural setting is an advantage. Experience in working with migrants, refugees, internally displaced persons, victims of trafficking and other vulnerable groups; Experience working in a multi\-cultural setting. Skills Advanced communication skills. Strong computer skills \- Word, Excel and Internet; Past experience with Movement Operations\-related databases and systems (including iGATOR, MiMOSA, SAR and Amadeus) is a distinct advantage. Good administrative, reporting, and organizational skills. Languages All IOM staff members in all categories are required to be fluent in one of the IOM's official languages (English, French, Spanish). For this position, Fluency in English (oral and written) is required. Proficiency of language(s) required will be specifically evaluated during the selection process, which may include written and/or oral assessments. Required Competencies IOM’s competency framework can be found at this link . Competencies will be assessed during the selection process. Values \- all IOM staff members must abide by and demonstrate these five values: Inclusion and respect for diversity : Respects and promotes individual and cultural differences. Encourages diversity and inclusion. Integrity and transparency : Maintains high ethical standards and acts in a manner consistent with organizational principles/rules and standards of conduct. Professionalism : Demonstrates ability to work in a composed, competent and committed manner and exercises careful judgment in meeting day\-to\-day challenges. Courage : Demonstrates willingness to take a stand on issues of importance. Empathy : Shows compassion for others, makes people feel safe, respected and fairly treated. Core Competencies – behavioural indicators Level 1 Teamwork : Develops and promotes effective collaboration within and across units to achieve shared goals and optimize results. Delivering results : Produces and delivers quality results in a service\-oriented and timely manner. Is action oriented and committed to achieving agreed outcomes. Managing and sharing knowledge : Continuously seeks to learn, share knowledge and innovate. Accountability : Takes ownership for achieving the Organization’s priorities and assumes responsibility for own actions and delegated work. Communication : Encourages and contributes to clear and open communication. Explains complex matters in an informative, inspiring and motivational way. Managerial Competencies – behavioural indicators Level 2, if with direct reports Leadership : Provides a clear sense of direction, leads by example and demonstrates the ability to carry out the Organization’s vision. Assists others to realize and develop their leadership and professional potential. Empowering others : Creates an enabling environment where staff can contribute their best and develop their potential. Building Trust : Promotes shared values and creates an atmosphere of trust and honesty. Strategic thinking and vision : Works strategically to realize the Organization’s goals and communicates a clear strategic direction. Humility : Leads with humility and shows openness to acknowledging own shortcomings. Notes Please refer to this link for guidance on IOM Job Category. Any offer made to the candidate in relation to this vacancy notice is subject to funding confirmation. This selection process may be used to staff similar positions in various duty stations. Recommended candidates will remain eligible to be appointed in a similar position for a period of 24 months. Appointment will be subject to certification that the candidate is medically fit for appointment, accreditation, any residency or visa requirements, security clearances. IOM has a zero\-tolerance policy on conduct that is incompatible with the aims and objectives of the United Nations and IOM, including sexual exploitation and abuse, sexual harassment, abuse of authority and discrimination based on gender, nationality, age, race, sexual orientation, religious or ethnic background or disabilities. IOM does not charge a fee at any stage of its recruitment process (application, interview, processing, training or other fee). IOM does not request any information related to bank accounts. IOM only accepts duly completed applications submitted through the IOM e\-Recruitment system (for internal candidates link here ). The online tool also allows candidates to track the status of their application. No late applications will be accepted. Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

title: Part Time Market Sales Assistant, Italian Food
description: We are Belmorso, an Italian food and olive oil business. We run weekend food markets and need a friendly, energetic person to sell our products to customers face to face. What you will do: set up and run our market stall, sell and talk to customers about our Italian food and olive oil, handle cash and card payments, and pack down at the end. You will work alongside the owner, who has run these markets for over 20 years, so full training is given. What we are looking for: someone outgoing who enjoys talking to people, reliable and punctual for early weekend starts, comfortable handling money, and happy to be on their feet outdoors. No experience needed, just the right attitude. A love of good food helps. Hours and pay: mainly weekends, Saturday and Sunday markets, a few hours per day. Pay from €15 per hour plus a performance bonus on strong sales days, so the more you sell the more you earn. Immediate start. Apply with a short note about yourself and your weekend availability. Pay: €14\.25\-€18\.00 per hour Work Location: In person

title: Partner Sales Executive - SMB UKI
description: *To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.* Job Category Sales Job Details **About Salesforce** Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. Accommodations If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form . title: Payment Operations Specialist
description: **Description** **TripleTen** is an EdTech company that designs and runs tech career learning programs for the US and Latin American markets. We've been doing it for over five years, teaching complete beginners — people with no prior tech background — through cohort\-based programs built on our own platform and curriculum, developed in partnership with Nebius AI. Our team is fully remote and globally distributed, and we serve a large, active student base across both regions. We are looking for a **Payments Operations Specialist** to support the end\-to\-end student payment lifecycle, including payment processing, loan operations, documentation management, refund handling, and cross\-functional coordination, while ensuring accuracy, efficiency, and an excellent student experience. **What You Will Do** * Manage and process student payments, ensuring accuracy across payment schedules, billing records, and partner systems. * Support loan operations by reviewing eligibility requirements, coordinating documentation, and maintaining accurate records throughout the approval process. * Maintain and validate payment data, reconcile transactions, and monitor enrollments to ensure data integrity across systems. * Process and manage student documentation, including enrollment agreements, academic records, and other operational documents. * Collaborate with internal teams to resolve payment\-related inquiries, refunds, documentation requests, and operational issues. * Handle payment operations workflows, including payment plan adjustments, program transfers, payment link generation, and issue resolution. * Calculate, process, and communicate student refunds in a timely and accurate manner. * Monitor recurring payment plans, proactively identifying and addressing payment issues to support a positive student experience. * Contribute to the continuous improvement of operational processes, documentation, and team workflows. **Please note:** The Payments Operations Team manages several operational streams. In this role, you will focus on a specific area while collaborating closely with the broader team. **Requirements** * 1\+ year of experience in an operational role within an IT, SaaS, EdTech, or other technology\-driven environment. * Advanced English communication skills, both written and spoken. * Advanced Excel skills, including complex formulas (e.g. XLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH), data validation, reporting, and working with large datasets. * Experience working with operational systems such as billing platforms, CRM tools, help desk systems, or data management tools. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills, with the ability to investigate issues, identify root causes, and make data\-driven decisions. * Excellent attention to detail and a high level of accuracy when working with financial, operational, and student\-related data. * Ability to manage multiple priorities simultaneously while maintaining strong organization and time\-management skills. * Comfort working with routine operational tasks while continuously identifying opportunities to improve processes and increase efficiency. * Ability to thrive in a fast\-paced environment with changing priorities and incomplete information. * Proactive mindset with a strong sense of ownership and accountability. * Experience using collaboration and documentation tools such as Slack, Notion, or similar platforms. * Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively across different teams and functions. * Interest in technology, online education, and digital products. * Experience building or documenting operational processes is a plus. * Familiarity with automation tools and workflow optimization is a plus. **What We Can Offer You** * Full\-time remote collaboration; * Compensation for part\-time collaboration is 600 USD per month. * A comfortable digital office. We use modern digital tools— Miro, Notion, Google Meet, etc.— to make the process of working together seamless. * Growth. Our product is expanding quickly, so it's a great time to get on board and challenge yourself. * Diverse and tight\-knit team which is spread out across Israel, Serbia, USA, Georgia, Armenia and more.

title: People Technology Integration Engineer (Workday)
description: **Why Sony Interactive Entertainment?** Sony Interactive Entertainment isn’t just the Best Place to Play — it’s also the Best Place to Work. Sony Interactive Entertainment (SIE) is the company behind the PlayStation brand. As a subsidiary of Sony Group Corporation, we’re part of a proud legacy of innovation and excellence. SIE is a dynamic technology company, delivering cutting\-edge hardware and network services to more than 100 million people and an entertainment leader, home to some of the most beloved and recognizable intellectual properties (IP) in the world. Our role at SIE is to create and nurture the experiences under the PlayStation brand, a name synonymous with entertainment excellence and creativity. **Role Overview:** The **People Technology Integration Engineer, Workday** is responsible for designing, building, and evolving integration and data solutions across SIE’s global Workday platform and broader HR technology ecosystem. This role operates as an independent contributor within People Technology Services, owning the design and delivery of scalable, secure, and reliable system integrations. The role focuses on enabling seamless data movement, system connectivity, and interoperability across platforms, ensuring that integrations are maintainable, performant, and aligned with architectural standards. Working within an Agile product delivery model, this role contributes to both the continuous evolution of integration capabilities and the stability of core data flows. The Integration Engineer partners closely with Platform Engineers, Product Managers, and Data teams to deliver cohesive, end\-to\-end technology solutions. This role is suited for individuals with strong technical depth who are motivated to build and improve integration architecture and data pipelines within an enterprise environment. **What you'll be doing:** **Integration Engineering \& Solution Design** * Design, build, and implement scalable integration solutions across the Workday platform and connected systems, including: * Workday Studio * Core Connectors and EIBs * API\-based integrations (REST/SOAP) * Define and apply integration design patterns that promote scalability, reusability, and maintainability * Design secure and reliable data flows, including transformation logic, orchestration, and error handling * Evaluate integration approaches and make informed design decisions, balancing business needs, system constraints, and long\-term sustainability * Own end\-to\-end delivery of integration solutions, including design, build, testing, and deployment readiness **Integration Architecture \& Platform Connectivity** * Contribute to the design and evolution of integration architecture across the People Technology ecosystem * Ensure consistent patterns for system connectivity, data exchange, and interface design * Document integration designs, data flows, and technical architecture to support maintainability and governance * Collaborate with multi\-functional teams to enable seamless end\-to\-end system interactions **Data Engineering \& Reporting Solutions** * Design and deliver data extracts, reporting solutions, and document generation capabilities (e.g., BIRT, Docs for Layouts) as part of the broader People data ecosystem * Develop and maintain calculated fields and data structures to support integration and reporting use cases * Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and consistency across integrations and downstream systems **Platform Reliability \& Operations** * Maintain reliability of integration pipelines through proactive monitoring, issue diagnosis, and resolution * Identify root causes of failures and implement structural improvements to enhance stability and performance * Optimize integrations for performance, scalability, and fault tolerance * Partner with team members to prioritize enhancements and manage competing demands **Delivery \& Agile Execution** * Translate business and technical requirements into well\-defined integration solutions * Participate actively in Agile delivery, including sprint planning, backlog refinement, and retrospectives * Own and deliver assigned workstreams within sprint cycles * Execute and support testing activities (unit, integration, UAT), ensuring end\-to\-end data validation * Collaborate with Platform Engineers and functional partners to deliver integrated, cross\-domain solutions **Governance, Security \& Quality** * Ensure all integration solutions meet data privacy, security, and audit requirements * Implement secure data exchange practices, including encryption, authentication, and secure file transfer (e.g., SFTP) * Follow established governance, release management, and change control practices * Ensure integration solutions align with platform architecture and engineering standards * Contribute to improving integration standards, documentation, and engineering practices **What we're looking for:** * 5\+ years of hands\-on experience with enterprise platforms, including design and delivery of Workday integrations * Demonstrated experience building and deploying integrations using: * Workday Studio * Core Connectors and EIBs * API\-based integrations (REST/SOAP) * Proven track record to design and troubleshoot integration solutions, including data transformation, interface logic, and failure handling * Experience implementing secure data exchange mechanisms, including encryption and secure file transfer * Hands\-on experience developing Workday reports (Advanced, Matrix, Composite) and calculated fields * Solid understanding of the Workday Core HCM data model and data flows across integrations and business processes * Experience translating requirements into scalable technical solutions, including evaluating trade\-offs between speed, complexity, and maintainability * Experience working in Agile delivery environments, with ownership of defined workstreams or sub\-projects * Ability to clearly articulate personal contributions to delivered solutions, including specific integrations and technical components **Preferred Qualifications** * Experience with Workday Orchestrate * Familiarity with Workday Security model * Exposure to Workday functional domains (e.g., Absence, Compensation, Talent) * Experience working within global enterprise environments across multiple regions * Experience contributing to integration standards, architecture, or engineering practices **Profile** * Strong engineering approach with deep technical expertise in integrations and data movement * Operates independently while aligning with architectural standards and team practices * Meticulous with a focus on data integrity, system reliability, and scalability * Practical and delivery\-focused, balancing speed, quality, and long\-term sustainability * Effective communicator who can bridge technical and functional perspectives * Comfortable working in an evolving environment, with a willingness to expand into new tools and technologies * Demonstrates curiosity, continuous learning, and a proactive approach to improving both individual capability and team outcomes At SIE, we consider several factors when setting each role’s base pay range, including the competitive benchmarking data for the market and geographic location. Please note that the base pay range may vary in line with our hybrid working policy and individual base pay will be determined based on job\-related factors which may include knowledge, skills, experience, and location. In addition, this role is eligible for SIE’s top\-tier benefits package that includes paid time off, wellness program and coveted employee discounts for Sony products. This role also may be eligible for a bonus package. Click **here** to learn more. The estimated base pay range for this role is listed below. €57\.900—€86\.900 EUR Please note, Sony Interactive Entertainment conducts background checks at the offer stage for all new employees (which may include criminal background checks for some roles) and will need to process personal information to support these checks. Please refer to our Candidate Privacy Notice for more information about what personal information we collect, how we use it, who we share it with, and your data protection rights. **Equal Opportunity Statement:** *Sony is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All persons will receive consideration for employment without regard to gender (including gender identity, gender expression and gender reassignment), race (including colour, nationality, ethnic or national origin), religion or belief, marital or civil partnership status, disability, age, sexual orientation, pregnancy, maternity or parental status, trade union membership or membership in any other legally protected category.* *We strive to create an inclusive environment, empower employees and embrace diversity. We encourage everyone to respond.* *Sony Interactive Entertainment is a Fair Chance employer and qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records will be considered for employment.*

title: Performing Arts Graduate- Artist in Residence
description: **Role Title:** Performing Arts Graduate – Artist in Residence. **Reporting to:** Director of Performing Arts **Summary Role Description:** The purpose of this role is to support the delivery of the school Performing Arts programme whilst giving a Graduate a platform to develop their recent area of study and gain valuable experience. This role offers accommodation and full board for the contract period. **Hours of Work:** 35 hours per week, over 5 days per week, for 36 weeks across term time. This role requires a great degree of flexibility, including working weekends, early mornings, late evenings, or accompanying students on overnight trips. The timetable for each week to be agreed with the Director of PA according to the needs of the department. **Role Description:** The successful candidate will join a dynamic and busy department where they will have the opportunity to develop their recently required studies and areas of interest while contributing to the successful running of the department. This is a residential opportunity, where you will have full board for the duration of the academic year as part of the remuneration package. · Project support: assist in the planning and execution of workshops, arts events and festivals. · Creative educator: assist in planning technique\-based curricula within your area of study of the Performing Arts. Team teach performing arts with the Head of Music/Director of performing Arts as well as contributing to the development of the content curriculum of the new DFTS course. · Assist in Directing the school production: assist in the planning, rehearsing and organisational aspects of producing a successful school production. · Evaluate Student progress: assist with formative assessments, including interviews, auditions and reports. · Preparation for Performance: prepare students for in\-house and external performances/competitions. · Assist in managing technical elements of modules/festivals/shows: depending on your area of expertise – stage lighting, sound design, costuming, set building, stage management. · Trip supervision: supervise students on external trips to competitions/festivals/events. · RIAM exam supervision: supervise students throughout the exam period. · Perform duties within the boarding life of the school that support the Performing Arts students’ development. **Qualifications and Essential Skills:** · Graduated within the last 3 years with an honours Degree level qualification, or above, in Drama, Theatre, Film \& TV or Musical Theatre. · Can demonstrate the ability to teach and inspire young adults in a classroom and/or workshop setting. · Can demonstrate technical skills (Lights, Sound, Camerawork, Music Production or Editing) in Theatre and/or Film. * Experience (in university, or otherwise) of directing or managing a show/festival/ large event. · Can demonstrate ability to independently create artistic work/projects to a high standard. · Excellent interpersonal, time management, organisational and project management skills and the ability to create good working partnerships. · Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office tools, particularly Excel, TEAMs and Word. **Desirable Skills:** · Piano Accompaniment for a number of areas: Musical rehearsals, student performances. · Singing proficiency: to assist with Musical theatre and choir rehearsals. · Knowledge of, or experience with, residential settings such as boarding schools or Youth residential camps. **Education and Qualifications:** The successful candidate is likely to have an honours Degree level qualification in Drama, Theatre, Film \& TV or Musical Theatre. **Graduate Role Selection Process:** · All applicants will be asked to provide a *Letter of Recommendation* from their most recent Educational Institution. Please ask your tutor/lecturer to include their contact details, as we will contact them if you are shortlisted for the role. · Please provide a cover letter stating both what you believe you can contribute to the King’s Hospital Performing Arts Programme and what you think you might gain from the role. · Please provide a CV, this can include a breakdown of areas studied and projects completed. · The short\-listed candidates will be asked to facilitate a 25 minute workshop with a group of students as well as a formal interview. **CHILD PROTECTION:** This post is subject to acceptable references and clearance from the Garda Vetting Bureau as part of the school’s legal requirement and commitment to providing a safe environment for our pupils. Pay: From €20,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Employee assistance program * On\-site gym * On\-site parking * Sick pay Application question(s): * Please note that the role includes accommodation and full board for the contract period. Work Location: In person

title: Pipeline Engineer.
description: At AIB, our values guide how we work and how we support each other. We’re looking for someone who puts **Customer First** , takes initiative and **Owns the Outcome** , and is always looking for ways to **Eliminate Complexity** . You’ll treat colleagues and customers with fairness and **Show Respect** , and you’ll thrive in a culture built on collaboration where we **Be One Team** to deliver meaningful impact. * Are you passionate about DevSecOps practices and excited by the world of automation? * Do you want to drive innovation and enable modern engineering standards across a large organisation? * Are you interested in reducing time‑to‑market by delivering robust, secure, and reliable CI/CD workflow solutions? **What Is The Role** The *Engineering and Enablement team,* part of Digital Platform organisation, is responsible for the delivery of Platform \& Technology Enablement, Quality Assurance, Framework \& Standards Leadership and Enterprise Apps Management for AIB. As a **Pipeline Engineer** , reporting to the Pipeline Engineering \& ETH Lead, you will play a key role in designing, building, and maintaining shared CI/CD workflows. You will work closely with other Centres of Expertise, including QA, Security, and Engineering, to implement engineering capabilities that support standardisation, efficiency, and overall delivery quality. The successful candidate will be technical, curious, and self‑driven, someone who enjoys learning, solving problems, and influencing the adoption of best‑practice and innovation DevSecOps patterns across the organisation. **Key Accountabilities** * Contribute to the DevOps and Automation Community of Practice. * Promote, integrate, and implement new DevOps tooling and strategic improvements. * Troubleshoot and resolve build and deployment issues as part of a platform\-focused team. * Collaborate closely with Development, Test, Infrastructure, Security, and Operations teams. * Mentor and support development teams in applying DevSecOps practices effectively. * Script complex automation solutions to eliminate repetitive manual tasks. * Drive initiatives that support a stable, scalable, and automated SDLC across multiple archetypes. **What You Will Bring** * 4\+ years' experience with Continuous Integration, build automation, and deployment tooling. * Strong understanding of DevOps principles and CI/CD methodologies. * Hands‑on experience with Jenkins \& Declarative Pipelines, or similar CI/CD tools (GitLab, GitHub Actions, etc.). * Experience using Git, Maven, Gradle, Groovy, Docker/Containerisation. * Solid understanding of all stages of the SDLC. * General knowledge of quality processes within development lifecycles. * Understanding of branching strategies and version control best practices. * Relevant third‑level IT qualification. * Experience working in an Agile delivery environment. * Strong communication and stakeholder‑influencing skills. * Preferred experience working with Azure cloud * Preferred experience working with Kubernetes environments * Preferred experience working in Java Enterprise application contexts * Preferred experience working in Configuration Management Tools such as Ansible **Why Work For AIB** We are committed to offering our colleagues choice and flexibility in how we work and live and our hybrid working model enables our people to balance their time between working from home and their designated office, subject to their role, the needs of our customers and business requirements. *Some of our benefits include;* Market leading Pension Scheme * Healthcare Scheme * Variable Pay * Employee Assistance Programme * Family leave options * Two volunteer days per year Please click **here** for further information about AIB’s PACT – Our Commitment to You. **Key Capabilities** * Behavioural + Eliminates Complexity + Promotes Innovation + Collaborates * Technical + Dev Sec Ops + Technical Support + Troubleshooting If you are not sure about your suitability based on any aspects of the role advertised, we encourage you to please contact the Recruiter for this role, Sarah Lyons, at careers@aib.ie for a conversation. AIB is an equal opportunities employer, and we pride ourselves on being the first bank in Ireland to receive the Investors in Diversity Gold Standard accreditation from the Irish Centre for Diversity. We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations for applicants and employees. Should you have a reasonable accommodation request please email the Talent Acquisition team at careers@aib.ie **Disclaimer** Unsolicited CV’s sent to AIB by Recruitment Agencies will not be accepted for this position. AIB operates a direct sourcing model and where agency assistance is required, the Talent Acquisition team will engage directly with our recruitment partners. **Application deadline: Thursday 2nd July 2026**

title: Platform Operations Specialist - Systems Infrastructure and Excellence - T&S
description: **Responsibilities** The Systems Infrastructure and Excellence (SIE) team plays a critical role in enhancing the operational capabilities and infrastructure of the Trust \& Safety (T\&S) organization. We focus on optimizing and automating workflows for T\&S systems to reduce operational costs and drive process excellence through strategic roadmapping and business prioritization. Our core functions include building robust tooling governance that aligns with compliance requirements and removing tooling fragmentation to create a more cohesive environment. We also empower moderation efficiency and quality with AI applications and collaborate with Operations, Engineering and Product teams to deliver user\-centric solutions. Additionally, we build common frameworks to integrate both human and machine moderation and deliver advanced business analytics to provide actionable insights. Responsibilities: \- User and Business Insight: Deeply understand business requirements by analyzing user feedback, conducting user research, and leveraging data to identify pain points and user needs. \- Data Strategy: Define and manage the data index system, identify key metrics, and drive data infrastructure improvements to support business optimization. \- Product Implementation: Translate business requirements into clear product documentation (MRD/BRD), develop platform and tool\-based solutions, and coordinate with R\&D teams to ensure successful implementation. \- Project Management: Proactively track and manage the progress of platform requirements, from testing and verification to launch and promotion with the business, to ensure timely and high\-quality delivery. \- User Enablement: Create and maintain platform operation documents, lead training sessions, and promote new features to boost user adoption and product understanding. \- Process Optimization: Continuously identify and improve and automate operational processes to enhance efficiency and effectiveness across the product development lifecycle. \- Cross\-Functional Collaboration: Serve as the primary liaison for cross\-functional teams (including R\&D, business, and legal), respond to urgent issues, and coordinate resources to ensure the smooth execution of global projects. **Qualifications** Minimum Qualifications \- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Business, Product Management, or a related technical/analytical field \- within 3 years of graduation \- Foundational understanding or experience with AI, LLMs, agents, and AI infrastructure. \- Strong ability to quickly understand complex business contexts, operational logic, and system architecture. \- Data\-driven mindset with the ability to leverage insights for business optimization. \- Exceptional logical thinking, problem\-solving, and business abstraction skills. \- Strong communication skills with the ability to collaborate effectively across product, business, and R\&D teams. Preferred Qualifications: \- Internship, placement year, early\-career, or professional experience in product, project, operations, or technical program management. \- Exposure to technical product development, automation systems, or AI\-driven business processes. \- Experience working cross\-functionally in fast\-paced, global environments. \- Additional relevant industry experience beyond early\-career stage is considered a plus. **Trust \& Safety** Content that this role interacts with includes images, video, and text related to every\-day life, but it can also include (but is not limited to) bullying; hate speech; child safety; depictions of harm to self and others, and harm to animals. Hence, it is possible that this role will be exposed to harmful content on a daily basis. ​ ​ TikTok recognises that keeping our platform safe for the TikTok communities is no ordinary job which can be both rewarding and psychologically demanding and emotionally taxing for some. This is why we are sharing the potential hazards, risks and implications in this unique line of work from the start, so our candidates are well informed before joining. ​ ​ We are committed to the wellbeing of all our employees and promise to provide comprehensive and evidence\-based programs, to promote and support physical and mental wellbeing throughout each employee's journey with us. We believe that wellbeing is a relationship and that everyone has a part to play, so we work in collaboration and consultation with our employees and across our functions in order to ensure a truly person\-centred, innovative and integrated approach.​ **About TikTok** TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​ **Why Join Us** Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day.​ We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.​ ​ Diversity \& Inclusion​ TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.​

title: Presales Solutions Architecture Consultant
description: Ergo have an exciting opportunity for a client\-facing consultative Solutions Architect to join our expanding Presales team. As a **Presales Solutions Architecture Consultant** , you will be the architectural North Star for your customers, working in lockstep with an Account Director to transform their business through technology. Unlike a focused pre\-sales role within a certain technology vertical (ie: Cloud, Digital, etc), this position requires a breadth of technology knowledge and is designed for a high\-impact technical strategist who thrives on building relationships within a select group of enterprise clients. This is a great time to join Ergo as we embrace a raft of new opportunities resulting from the Presidio merger. You will report directly into our newly appointed VP of Presales. We're happy to consider **remote** candidates but you should be open to **Dublin\-based** work when required where many of our clients are based. **What will you do?** * Act as the **Trusted Advisor** for a small portfolio of high\-value "Focus Accounts," understanding their business goals as intimately as their technical stack. * Develop multi\-year **Technology Roadmaps** that align Managed Services (continuity) with Professional Services (transformation). * Support your Account director in running strategic quarterly business reviews to present innovation opportunities and risk mitigation strategies. * Assist the Account Director in the design and delivery of various customer briefing sessions * Lead the discovery and design phases for complex, multi\-vendor solutions spanning Cloud, Infrastructure, Cybersecurity, EUC and Digital technologies. * Bridge the gap between business requirements and technical delivery, ensuring all proposed solutions are commercially viable and operationally supportable. * Own the creation of high\-quality **Statements of Work (SoW)** , High\-Level Designs (HLD), and technical proposals for all core service offerings. * Working closely with Presales SME Solution Architects to create **Statements of Work (SoW)** , High\-Level Designs (HLD), and technical proposals for all non\-core service offerings. * Partner with the Account Director to identify white\-space opportunities and drive additional lines of business within your chosen accounts. * Work with the Account Director to build robust **Business Cases** and ROI models to help customers justify large\-scale technology investments. * Work with the Account Director, Sales Specialists and Presales SMEs to both influence, shape and respond to RFI's and RFP's. * Work with Managed services to size, scope and price Managed services proposals within your existing customer base * Provide guidance and peer reviews for junior staff members within the team. * Stay at the forefront of industry trends, acting as a feedback loop between our customers and our internal Product/Service Development teams. **What do you need?** * 10\+ years in a Pre\-Sales, Solutions Architecture, or Technical Consulting role * Proven expertise across Public/Hybrid Cloud, Modern Networking, Cybersecurity frameworks, and Managed Service operations * Strategic Mindset with the ability to pivot from "fixing a problem" to "enabling a business outcome" * Exceptional presentation skills and C\-level gravitas Apply today for more information.

title: Process Engineer
description: **PE Global is recruiting for a Process Engineer for our biopharmaceutical client in Dublin. This is an initial 12\-month contract, hybrid role.** **Job Description:** This role will require the candidate to provide Process Development support for syringe filling and Single use technologies development for drug product Formulation \& Filling processes. Key upcoming projects under this scope include characterisation and qualification of filling technology and new single use technology for 3 new syringe filling lines as part of the ADL expansion project (ADL1X) and new plant introduction ADL2\. The candidate will provide technical support to large molecule and small molecule drug product manufacturing at ADL as well as part of the global Process Development (PD) organization. **Specific responsibilities include but are not limited to:** * Developing single use systems in collaboration with ADL site teams and network for DP formulation and filling. * Authoring / verification of single use qualification packages. * Filling characterisation support (Filling recipe development \- protocol development / execution and reporting.) * Demonstrated ability in providing leadership to cross\-functional teams to advance complex projects to completion. Excellent Communication skills are essential for this role, as well as a demonstrated proficiency with the principles of Technical Writing. * Leads and conducts risk assessment for drug product operations and propose / implement appropriate CAPA. * Provide process development expertise for drug product processing in specific areas such as sterile processing, process characterization, filling, SUS. Support commercial drug product manufacturing operations with technical evaluation of NC/CAPA and technology transfer. Interfaces with manufacturing as well as all support functions to provide robust and coordinated support to manufacturing. * Troubleshoots issues with drug product processing technologies and equipment. Champions use of data\-driven analyses and visualizations. Investigates to determine root cause. * Lead or assist in deviation / exception resolution and root cause analysis. * Responsibilities may include leading cross\-site teams to support the above activities, providing recommendations to management. * Ensure that the site meets the quality requirements of its customers and applicable regulations (FDA, EU \& international standards). * Review and post\-approve process validation protocols and assessments from a quality system documentation perspective. * Contribute to product quality assessments and process flow documents. * Liaise with Operations, Engineering and external vendors regarding new or amended equipment which would impact the validated process. **Basic Qualifications:** * A third level Bachelor’s Degree in Science, Engineering or a relevant Quality discipline with 5 years’ experience in a similar role OR Master’s degree \& 3 years of directly related experience OR Associate’s degree \& 10 years of directly related experience. * Knowledge of cGMPs and other worldwide regulatory requirements. * Thorough knowledge of aseptic manufacturing technologies and cGMP. * Problem solving ability and excellent oral and written communications skills * Strong skills in applying fundamental engineering and scientific principles to the design and implementation of Formulation Single use systems and Filling **.** **Preferred Qualifications:** * 10 \+ years of experience in support of Commercial Protein Drug Product (DP) processing in specific areas such as Sterile Processing, Process Characterization, Tech Transfer (to commercial DP sites) or Validation. * Strong skills in applying fundamental engineering and scientific principles to the design, implementation and process validation of formulation, mixing, filtration and filling. Knowledge of protein biochemistry regarding chemical and physical stability * Background in Drug product Filling technologies, Peristaltic pumping / Time pressure filling. * Strong knowledge of Quality systems, Drug Product Manufacturing and Validation. * Demonstrated ability in providing leadership to cross\-functional teams to advance complex projects to completion. Excellent Communication skill is essential for this role. **Interested candidates should submit an updated CV.** **Please click the link below to apply, or alternatively send an up\-to\-date CV to paul.wheatley@peglobal.net** **\*\*\*Please note our client cannot assist with any visa sponsorship and candidates must have the correct visa to live and work in Ireland\*\*\***

title: Production / Application Support
description: **Responsibilities** * Act as a liaison between business, IT and QA teams to ensure alignment on requirements and technical deliverables. * Interacting with functional BAs, product SMEs, QAs, engineering teams and solution architects to provide walk through of the requirements, understand technical dependencies and work with them to deliver the solutions. * Provide content leadership in working with the clients in defining solutions to their business requirements. * Interacting with business stakeholders for requirements gathering, analysis, documentation etc. * Work with the delivery / implementation teams to provide domain inputs and to ensure delivery and implementation of the proposed solution. * Interacting with the technical team to ensure that the requirements are clear and well understood. * Translating business requirement document into technical specifications and analyse payment\-related requirements. * Oversee or participate in UAT and resolve technical issues during development and post\-implementation. * Relevant Industry experience, having worked in Tier\-1 IT organizations in BFSI domain with a focus on Payments Domain experience in Low and High Value Payments, Cross Border Payments and key regulations \& associated IT impacts, affecting the Payments market, such as Single Euro Payments Area (SEPA), Instant Payments, Payment Services Directive, Open Banking etc. **Required Skillset** **Domain Expertise:** * Must have in\-depth solutioning experience in Banking Domain with focus on payments. * Good to have the ability to read and understand regulatory changes such as SWIFT / SEPA rulebooks and understand requirements. * Must know SQL/Oracle databases **Technical Skills:** * Should have the ability to understand As\-is and gather requirements. * Knowledge of payment system and technologies (ISO 20022, EMV, tokenization) * Familiarity with Database, scripting, Microservice architecture.

title: Production Support Analyst
description: At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts. Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet. Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all. **About The Team And The Role** The Global Payments Operations Tech team is responsible for ensuring the stability, reliability, and scalability of the Payments 2\.0 Platform. As a Production Support Analyst, you will work at the intersection of technology and operations, supporting critical Payments, Billing, Collections, and Finance systems. This role focuses on modernizing production support by using observability, automation, and operations enhanced with artificial intelligence. It helps improve system resiliency, speeds incident response, and lowers manual intervention. You will partner closely with engineering, operations, and business teams while serving as a key operational expert across financial systems and processes. **What You Will Accomplish** * Partner with multi\-functional teams to define and improve the production support roadmap across Payments, Billing, Collections, and Finance platforms. * Lead production support activities, including incident response, escalation management, troubleshooting, and resolution coordination for business\-critical systems. * Design and implement AI\-powered automation solutions that improve monitoring capabilities, enable proactive issue detection, and reduce incident resolution times. * Build and maintain intelligent triage and auto\-remediation workflows by integrating operational data sources, alerts, logs, and runbooks. * Drive improvements to platform observability, resiliency, and production stability through monitoring enhancements, process optimization, and automation initiatives. * Support production releases, system validations, month\-end close activities, audits, and compliance requirements, including adherence to SOX IT controls. * Develop operational documentation, dashboards, and standard operating procedures while contributing to continuous improvement initiatives across the production environment. **What You Will Bring** * Experience supporting large\-scale Payments, Billing, Financial Systems, or similar business\-critical platforms. * Deep technical skills in UNIX/Linux environments, Python and Shell scripting, and SQL/NoSQL databases including Oracle PL/SQL and MongoDB. * Experience with workflow orchestration tools such as Apache Airflow and familiarity with data platforms including Data Warehousing, Hadoop, or other Big Data technologies. * Hands\-on experience with observability and monitoring tools such as Grafana, Kibana, and Splunk, combined with strong analytical and troubleshooting capabilities. * Experience applying AI/ML and/or LLM\-based solutions in production support environments, including monitoring, incident triage, root cause analysis, and automated resolution workflows. * Knowledge of leveraging LLMs for log analysis, incident investigation, knowledge retrieval, and operational automation; experience with AI\-assisted operations tools is preferred. * Good communication, organizational, and documentation skills, with the ability to work effectively in a fast\-paced global support environment. This role includes participation in an on\-call rotation for weekend and off\-hours production support and may require support for critical incidents, urgent escalations, and production releases. **Additional Details** eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines.

title: Production Support Engineer
description: **Overview** As a Production Support Engineer, you will join a highly technical, business\-critical Production Support team responsible for maintaining and enhancing trading systems in a fast\-paced environment. The team plays a key role in ensuring system stability, scalability, and performance across global trading operations. You will collaborate closely with traders, development, and infrastructure teams to support live trading environments, respond to incidents, and drive continuous improvement in system reliability, monitoring, and deployment practices. This is a role combining production support and engineering, requiring both strong troubleshooting capability and the ability to build, automate, and improve systems. **In This Role You Will** * Provide technical support to trading environments, troubleshooting complex issues and resolving incidents in a time\-sensitive setting. * Build and improve automation tools to reduce manual intervention and increase system reliability. * Deploy, maintain, and monitor custom trading applications to ensure stability, performance, and availability. * Contribute to and deliver project work, including system optimisation, configuration improvements, and infrastructure upgrades. * Act as a key point of contact between development, infrastructure, and business teams to support production. * Diagnose and resolve application and system issues through log analysis and investigation of system behaviour and workflows. * Maintain and enhance monitoring and alerting tools to proactively detect and respond to issues. * Review incidents to identify trends and implement improvements to increase automation and operational efficiency. * Develop and maintain documentation, support procedures, and best practices. * Assist in managing system capacity, resilience, and scalability to support business growth. **What We're Looking For** * 5\+ years of experience in production engineering, development or a similar technical role. * Hands\-on experience with scripting or development (e.g. Python, Powershell or similar) with an automation and tooling focus. * Strong troubleshooting and analytical skills, with the ability to investigate issues through logs and system behaviour. * Experience working with distributed systems, including understanding of networking fundamentals and system interactions. * Experience with scripting or automation (e.g. Python, PowerShell, or similar). * Exposure to monitoring and alerting tools (e.g. ELK stack, CheckMk, Nagios). * Familiarity with database systems and data\-driven applications. * Experience working with ticketing systems and managing multiple concurrent support requests effectively while under pressure. * Strong communication skills, with the ability to clearly explain technical concepts to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders. * Willing to support shift work and on\-call responsibilities. * Experience in financial systems or trading environments is beneficial but not essential. Susquehanna is an equal opportunity employer. If you need assistance and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application or recruitment process, please contact us.

title: Property Services Administration Officer
description: **Role Overview** * Position: Property Services Administration Officer * Reporting to: Property Services Administrative Team Leader * Location: Northbank House, Dublin 1 (Hybrid) * Contract: Fixed\-term until 31st December 2026 * Salary: €32,584 – €44,803 per annum (DOE) **The Operations Department:** The Operations Department is dedicated to delivering essential services to our residents, including allocations, rent calculation and collection, and tenancy management. Additionally, we provide estate services and support community development activities to promote well\-kept, friendly neighbourhoods. **P** **roperty Services Administration Officer** Your role will be to assist the Administrative Team Leader and provide administrative support nationally across all stock and locations. You will carry out a range of administrative roles that will add real value to the service and support the team to deliver on its key objectives. You will be working with a range of colleagues across the business, and you will have considerable interaction with our residents and contractors alike. Through excellent organisation and work prioritisation there are real opportunities to get great job satisfaction from making a visible difference to colleagues and residents alike. **Key Responsibilities** **Servicing \& Compliance** * Oversee the administration of servicing programmes for residents’ homes. * Liaising with contractors to meet deadlines and escalate any problems. * Handle inbound queries, ensuring all resident requests are managed efficiently. * Update and maintain servicing and repair records. * Conduct quality checks on documentation for compliance. * Preparation of regular compliance reports. * Liaise with the Residential Tenancies Board (RTB) for non\-access cases. **General Office Administration** * Data entry, data management, and reporting from stock and repairs records. * Liaise with residents, internal teams, and contractors. * Assist with surveys, snag reports, stock ordering, and minute\-taking. * Assist in management of multiple busy inboxes. * Follow up with stakeholders for required information. * Manage invoices and credit card reconciliation. * Oversee national fleet operations, liaising with fleet management and staff. **Collaborative Working** * Work across teams to enhance operational efficiency and community outcomes. **Complaints \& Residence Voice** * Prioritise resident feedback to improve services. * Work with management to resolve complaints in line with Clúid’s policy. * Support remedial actions and continuous improvement initiatives. **Corporate Responsibilities** * Align activities with Clúid’s values and mission. * Adhere to company policies and procedures. * Maintain discretion and confidentiality. * Promote health, safety, and wellbeing in all tasks. **Person Specification** * Strong interpersonal skills * Excellent attention to detail * Highly organised * Strong IT skills **Experience/Qualifications** * 3\+ Years’ Experience in a similar role * Experience in repairs, construction, or customer facing services * Minimum Leaving Certificate * Knowledge of Fleet Management * GDPR knowledge * Excellent knowledge of Microsoft suite or similar * Experience in managing computer filing systems * Dealing with customers both internal and external or demonstrable customer focused skills * Data Entry, Management \& Manipulation experience **About Us** Clúid is at the forefront of providing affordable, quality housing in Ireland, with a vision to create communities where everyone has a great place to live. We're looking for talented individuals passionate about making a tangible impact in people's lives and eager to contribute to social change. **Why Choose Clúid?** At Clúid, we believe that success is achieved through our dedicated and passionate team. Working with us, you'll enjoy: * Dynamic Working Environment: Thrive in a supportive, innovative, and collaborative workspace. * Flexible Working Arrangements: We support work\-life balance with flexible working options with Hybrid working options. * Comprehensive Training and Development Opportunities: Grow professionally with our continuous learning opportunities. * Employee Benefits: 25 days annual leave \+3 additional days over Christmas and Good Friday, 10% Pension, VHI, Employee Assistance Programme, Bike to work, Tax Saver Travel, Sick Pay, Payment Income Protection, Paid Maternity Leave, Paid Paternity Leave. * Inclusive Culture: Be part of an organisation that values diversity and inclusion, recognised with a Gold Award from Investors for Diversity.

title: Prosper Group Careers Open Evening
description: Are you looking for a rewarding career where you can make a real difference in the lives of others? Prosper Group is hosting a Careers Open Evening on **Wednesday 24 June 2026 from 4pm\-7pm at The Obelisk, Skerries, Co. Dublin.** Prosper Fingal and Prosper Meath are part of Prosper Group, supporting over 500 adults with intellectual disabilities and autism across North Dublin and Meath. Dublin Register your place via Eventbrite: https://www.eventbrite.ie/e/1989599027249?aff\=oddtdtcreator We look forward to welcoming you.

title: Quantitative Data Operations Engineer
description: **Overview** At Susquehanna, we’ve always thrived on staying ahead of the market. While speed and complexity defined the past, today data is the frontier for unlocking trading edge. Our European **Quantitative Engineering and Data (QED) group** is expanding rapidly, creating powerful algorithms, datasets, and platforms that fuel revenue generation. Based in Dublin and collaborating with teams in London and across the globe, we work at the intersection of **Engineering, Operations, and Research** . We’re building scalable, high\-performance systems that transform how traders and researchers use data — and we’re looking for passionate engineers to join us. **The Role** As a **Quantitative Data Operations Engineer** , you’ll be at the centre of our trading and research ecosystem. You’ll partner directly with traders, researchers, and engineers to ensure that operation of our data platforms meets the business needs. This isn’t a passive support role — you’ll spend a significant portion of your time writing production scripts and code a well as wrangling our systems to produce the data we need for trading and research. We don’t pretend our systems are perfect, in fact when you are at the bleeding edge the one constant is change and unplanned work. You will need to be able to navigate this ambiguity and feedback to engineers your insights into how our platforms can evolve. **What You’ll Do** * Operate critical research data systems used daily by traders and quants (Airflow and proprietary data systems). * Monitor data systems in US and EMEA ensuring business readiness as part of our follow\-the\-sun support model. * Orchestrate data pipelines \& dependencies e.g. compute cluster, storage, config, data locality. * Be the first point of contact for researchers and traders — helping to remove blockers and accelerate the business. * Participate in a daily rota in a rolling early and late shift pattern, and an on\-call rota. * Use AI and agents to remove toil that frees up time for system improvements. * Champion best practices, ensuring changes are reliable, performant, and globally consistent. * Lead incident post\-mortems and follow up on learnings. **What We're Looking For** * A degree in a STEM discipline. * Scripting skills in Python and bash. * Experience with orchestration tools such as Airflow is a plus. * Comfort working in a Linux environment. * Strong problem\-solving mindset with a drive to unblock peers and improve systems. * Ability to balance short\-term demands with long\-term architecture improvements. * Collaborative, communicative, and detail\-oriented. * Interest in quantitative trading and financial markets. **Why Join Us?** * Impact at scale: Your code will directly support trading and research across global markets. * Growth \& learning: Gain deep exposure to trading, quantitative research, and large\-scale data engineering. * Cutting\-edge challenges: Work on high\-performance, big data systems in a mission\-critical environment. * Collaborative culture: Partner with world\-class quants, traders, and engineers. * Career acceleration: This is a high\-visibility role at the heart of a rapidly expanding team.

title: Quantitative Sports Trader
description: **Overview** This is a trading and quantitative strategy role focused primarily on trading on prediction markets. You will make fast, informed trading decisions while developing and maintaining the quantitative models, data pipelines, and tools that drive trading performance. The role blends hands\-on trading with quantitative research, model development, and product ownership — ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of markets, analytics, and technology. **In This Role, You Will** * Trade using in\-house models, data\-driven insights, and automated tools to maximize expectancy and meet business objectives. * Take ownership of trading strategies and associated models — designing, implementing, and refining logic for execution, pricing, and market\-making. * Collaborate closely with quantitative researchers and engineers to identify, design, and deploy model enhancements, data features, and trading infrastructure improvements. * Perform post\-trade analysis of decisions, model performance, and market behavior to inform strategy development and model calibration. * Identify opportunities in prediction market dynamics — pricing inefficiencies, liquidity behavior, or reaction times — to strengthen the team’s edge. * Drive innovation in trading methodology, tooling, and data usage across the trading and quantitative teams. **What We're Looking For** * Bachelor’s degree or higher in a technical or quantitative discipline (e.g., Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Physics, Economics). * Deep domain expertise and passion for sports such as Football, Soccer or Golf, including understanding of tactical, statistical, and market aspects of the sport and direct experience trading them. * Demonstrated ability in Python, including strong experience with data manipulation (Pandas, NumPy), statistical analysis, and automation. * Experience integrating data and models into production trading workflows is a strong plus. * Exceptional quantitative reasoning, analytical, and problem\-solving skills. * Experience trading or modelling in fast\-moving, competitive markets (prediction markets, exchanges, or similar). * Ability to operate across both trading and quantitative research, bridging analytical insight with execution. * Comfort taking product ownership — defining priorities, implementing features, and improving workflows end\-to\-end. * Strong communication skills, both for collaboration and articulating decision rationale. * Flexibility with working hours to align with global game/match/tournament schedules. If you're a recruiting agency and want to partner with us, please reach out to recruiting@sig.com. Any resume or referral submitted in the absence of a signed agreement will not be eligible for an agency fee.

title: Quantity Surveyor
description: We are Cunningham. Founded in 2006, Cunningham is one of the UK's and Europe's leading construction companies. With extensive operations across multiple markets, we have built a strong reputation for innovation, quality, and safety, delivering successful projects through a proactive, collaborative approach. Our expertise spans a diverse range of sectors, including Mission Critical \& Life Sciences, Education, Healthcare, Fit Out, Residential, and Civils \& Structural projects. Today, Cunningham employs more than 280 professionals across offices in Ireland, Germany, and the UK. Working in partnership with our clients, we unlock the full potential of every project, transforming ambitious visions into successful outcomes while creating exciting opportunities for our people. At Cunningham you will be given the opportunity to excel in your career. Our people set us apart, and that’s why we invest in your journey from day one. Your expertise, ambition, and commitment will be recognised and rewarded as you build a successful career with us. We are now seeking and experienced and talented **Quantity Surveyor** to join our high performing team. **REPORTING TO:** Senior QS **ROLE PURPOSE:** The Quantity Surveyor is responsible for providing commercial support to the project team, maintaining commercial information and ensuring compliance with contractual requirements. The Quantity Surveyor will liaise with customer representatives and other third parties on commercial issues including agreement of variations, claims and any additional payments. **Role Responsibilities** * Ensure robust, accurate and timely cost and value reporting at both project and business unit level. * Ensure accurate cash flow reporting takes place. * Carry out cost management including forecasting. * Update the monthly CVR, cost and commercial plans and have accountability, along with the project team for the commercial performance and strategy. * Ensure that any sub\-contractors have agreed terms and conditions that are appropriate for Cuningham Contracts, with commercial risks identified. * Ensure that the supply chain are engaged appropriately with the correct contract terms and conditions. * Ensure that good client relationships are fostered. * Be responsible for subcontract letting, negotiation and financial accounting. * Ensure effective interaction between the commercial team and the operational site teams. * Help ensure that commercial processes are adhered to across the Commercial department. * Have a good understanding of the processes and procedures of the Commercial department, and actively seek to assist in development and improvement where possible. * Coach and mentor Junior Quantity Surveyors within the Commercial department, sharing knowledge and best practice. * Act as mentor to any apprentices that may be attached to the Commercial department, sharing knowledge and best practice, acting as a point of contact for any queries, and providing feedback on the apprentice as required. **Knowledge, Skills \& Experience Required** * Degree level qualification in Quantity Surveying, or equivalent. * A minimum of 4 years post grad experience in the Construction industry, working with a contractor * Previous experience on Residential projects in Ireland is desirable * Familiarity with various form of contract, Public works Ireland is desirable * Extensive experience in commercial management and procurement of subcontractors, including chairing regular meetings, measurement and control cycle, certificates and accruals calculations. * Ability to demonstrate a good knowledge of monthly reporting and earned value analysis. * Excellent team player whilst also holding the ability to work independently on own initiative. * Excellent time management skills, with ability to work to tight deadlines. **Additional information** * All candidates must have the Right to Work in the UK and in the Republic of Ireland at the time of application. Proof of this will be required for any candidate who is successful in the role. **Benefits** As part of the Cunningham team, you will be offered the following employee benefits in addition to your market leading salary: **Work\-Life \& Wellbeing** 31 days Annual Leave Early finish on Fridays Flexible Working Policy Life Assurance Policy Company Occupational Health Employee Assistance Programme Wellbeing Events **Development \& Support** Ongoing Training \& Development Team Development Days Support for Chartership Pathways Bespoke Career Pathways Professional Membership Support **Company Culture \& Perks** State\-of\-the\-art office facilities On\-site Car Parking Branded clothing Social Events *Cunningham is an equal opportunities employer and we welcome applications from all suitably qualified persons.*

title: Ready to start a Career in Recruitment Consulting?
description: * **Industry\-leading training program:** Transition smoothly into recruitment with a step\-by\-step learning and achievement journey. * **Unlimited potential for growth:** Share your financial security with your loved ones * **Collaborative environment:** Your success is your team’s success We at **Access Healthcare** are yet again expanding and looking for creative and energetic individuals to join us as **Recruitment Consultants** in our Dublin office (full training provided) **About the Job** As a Recruitment Consultant, you will play a key role in connecting healthcare professionals with leading employers, building strong client and candidate relationships, and managing the full recruitment lifecycle in a fast\-paced, target\-driven environment. **What We Offer** * **Fast\-track clear career progression** with the opportunity to earn multiple promotions within 12–24 months * **Uncapped commission and rapid earning potential** directly linked to your performance (Year 1 expectations on average for **Basic salary \+ Unlimited commission is €45K\+;** Year 2: **€75K\+** ) * **Merit\-based advancement** \- promotions based purely on results, not time served * **Social and energetic workplace culture** — making work feel more engaging and rewarding **What we’re looking for** * At least 1 year of post\-graduation experience, ideally in sales or client\-facing roles * Confident communicator with strong relationship\-building skills * Ability to commute to Dublin 2, Monday to Friday * Ambitious, self\-motivated, and driven to exceed expectations * EU Passport or Stamp 4 Visa required For more information, contact me at daniel.moreira@accesshealthcare.ie or call \+ (353\) 1 572 1201\.
